data.text: Using the `ddx` workflow here because this is a bead execution task and the contract is tied to tracker evidence. I’m going to inspect the existing docs and the axon TD first, then draft the single docs artifact if the scope is truly one-file sized.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "pwd && rg --files docs .ddx | sed -n '1,200p' && printf '\\n---\\n' && rg -n \"axon|bd|doltdb|bead backend|backend-migration\" docs .ddx -g '"'!**/node_modules/**'"'"

docs/adaptive-min-tier.md:1:Removed in ddx-3bd7396a. AdaptiveMinTier and related helpers are deleted.
docs/bead-backends.md:3:This document describes the bd/DoltDB fallback path for bead storage if the axon rollout stalls or is deferred. It is the practical wiring guide for preserving the `Backend` contract introduced in `ddx-bbdd7564` while contrasting that path with the Axon backend design in [TD-030](helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md).
docs/bead-backends.md:19:That separation is what lets DDx keep JSONL as a safe default while swapping in bd, br, or axon behavior without changing call sites.
docs/bead-backends.md:21:## Where The bd Path Lives
docs/bead-backends.md:23:The bd/br path is wired today through `cli/internal/bead/store.go:88-137` and `cli/internal/bead/backend_external.go:10-149`.
docs/bead-backends.md:26:- When the backend type is `bd` or `br`, `Store` constructs `ExternalBackend`.
docs/bead-backends.md:30:If a future implementation wants a more explicit bd adapter, it should live beside these files in `cli/internal/bead/`, for example as `backend_bd.go`, but it should still satisfy `RawBackend` and preserve the same `Backend` surface.
docs/bead-backends.md:34:The axon path is defined in `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:15-105` and selected in `cli/internal/bead/store.go:127-137`.
docs/bead-backends.md:38:- The current implementation is an in-process emulation that persists two JSONL collections under `.ddx/axon/`.
docs/bead-backends.md:40:That makes axon a feature-flagged, repository-local implementation path. The bd fallback is different:
docs/bead-backends.md:42:- bd is an external dependency.
docs/bead-backends.md:43:- bd is selected by backend configuration rather than an experiment flag.
docs/bead-backends.md:44:- bd uses the same JSONL interchange contract for import/export.
docs/bead-backends.md:45:- bd inherits DoltDB's git-for-data model, so the storage semantics are repository-backed and branch/commit oriented rather than purely local files.
docs/bead-backends.md:47:## Future Wiring Steps For A bd Fallback
docs/bead-backends.md:49:If axon adoption stalls and bd becomes the preferred backend, a future implementer should follow these steps:
docs/bead-backends.md:53:3. Route `bead.backend: bd` or `DDX_BEAD_BACKEND=bd` to the bd adapter in `NewStore`.
docs/bead-backends.md:54:4. Implement the bd adapter in `cli/internal/bead/` as a `RawBackend`-compatible type, or keep reusing `ExternalBackend` if the shell-out contract remains sufficient.
docs/bead-backends.md:56:6. Preserve the JSONL fallback for collections that bd cannot serve natively, using the same pattern currently documented in `backend_external.go`.
docs/bead-backends.md:57:7. Keep the chaos/conformance suite pointed at the `Backend` interface so bd and JSONL exercise the same contract.
docs/bead-backends.md:61:The bd path is attractive because it aligns with the upstream bead-format ecosystem, but it carries an external dependency:
docs/bead-backends.md:63:- the `bd` binary must be installed and available in `PATH`
docs/bead-backends.md:64:- the fallback depends on the bd codebase and its DoltDB runtime, not just on DDx's repository
docs/bead-backends.md:66:- operator environments need a clear fallback story when `bd` is missing
docs/bead-backends.md:68:That is the main difference from axon in this repository:
docs/bead-backends.md:70:- axon is self-contained inside DDx but is still experimental
docs/bead-backends.md:71:- bd is stable as a storage option but depends on an external tool and DoltDB's git-for-data semantics
docs/bead-backends.md:75:If axon remains unstable, prefer this ordering:
docs/bead-backends.md:78:2. Use bd for operators who want Dolt-backed storage and already have the binary installed.
docs/bead-backends.md:79:3. Keep axon behind `DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL` until its conformance and rollout are proven.

docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:18:The name follows the `bd` (Dolt-backed) and `br` (SQLite-backed) convention: short, lowercase tool names for the same conceptual unit. DDx beads use JSONL-backed local storage; `bd` and `br` provide database-backed alternatives. All three interchange via JSONL.

docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:112:For `bd` and `br` backends, DDx shells out to the respective binary. For `jsonl`, DDx manages the file directly.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:128:The bead backend is the reusable DDx storage engine for bead-schema records.
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:141:Following the bd convention, the prefix is **derived from the repository name** by default:
docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:148:This matches how bd derives its prefix, ensuring beads created by DDx and bd in the same repo use the same naming convention.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-fdd3ea36/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes\nrationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)\n\n## Decision\n\nStopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a\nmulti-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it\nin one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that\nfails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config\nschema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs\nwithout the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.\n\n## What is already done (verified in this worktree)\n\n1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under\n   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in\n   `closed` status:\n     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)\n     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)\n     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)\n     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)\n     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)\n     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)\n\n2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**\n   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.\n   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns\n   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)\n   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1\n   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.\n\n## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass\n\n### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative\n\nAC #1 asks for \"a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class\ndefault and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +\ngating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost\ndimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback.\"\n\nReading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:\n  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.\n  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.\n  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,\n    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent\n    \"routing point release\" narrative.\n\nThe closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as\nside-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the\ncombined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires\neither (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a \"v0.9.25 — routing\npoint release\" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead\ndescription must be relaxed to accept \"the code is in v0.9.23, even if\nno single CHANGELOG block summarizes it.\" Either resolution is a\ngovernance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.\n\n### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)\n\nThese criteria collectively require:\n  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one\n    `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` call with no Model / no Harness /\n    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;\n  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim\n    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);\n  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,\n    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,\n    with one-time process warnings;\n  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to\n    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;\n  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /\n    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with\n    dead-code detection in CI;\n  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn\n    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override\n    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness\n    conflict);\n  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's\n    real config;\n  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.\n\nThis is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:\n  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`\n  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)\n  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)\n  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)\n  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)\n\nThat work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead\nexplicitly bundles into \"one DDx PR\" — but a PR of that size needs\narchitectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking\nconfig change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the\nfirst line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC\ndeterministically, not to make architecture decisions.\n\n### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads\n\nThe bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:\n  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,\n  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)\n    stays independent.\n\nFiling beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the\nproject's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is\nmechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.\n\n### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden\n\nA net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical\n19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test\n(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not\nhave gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt needs\n\nTo make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead\nmust be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:\n\n1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever\n   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add\n   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially\n   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.\n\n2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default\n   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:\n   \"default\")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.\n\n3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before\n   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded\n   incompatibility.\n\n4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on\n   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +\n   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.\n\n5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override\n   combination.\n\n6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test\n   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.\n\n7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of\n   compensating logic.\n\n8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.\n\n9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI\n   bead.\n\nEach of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The\ncurrent bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one\nattempt.\n\n## Recommendation to the orchestrator\n\nReopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into\nthe nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three\n(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated\ngoals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.\n\nThe reduced-scope language in the bead's own description (\"DDx-side\ntracker only\", \"blocks on a single upstream point release\") suggests\nthis bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as\nthe deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.\nresult_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nbase_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nretry_after=2026-04-29T04:30:22Z","created_at":"2026-04-28T22:30:22.575970819Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"no_changes"}
.ddx/attachments/ddx-fdd3ea36/events.jsonl:6:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes\nrationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)\n\nThis is the second consecutive no_changes for this bead (prior attempt\n20260428T222708-3b6ff345 reached the same conclusion). I re-verified the\nupstream / DDx state and concur with that prior analysis: the bead is a\nmulti-PR epic, not a single bounded execute-bead pass, and it should be\nsplit before another implementation attempt.\n\n## What is verified done\n\n1. All six upstream routing-plan beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` are\n   `closed`:\n     agent-0dafc7f0, agent-191a74f9, agent-1f46cf22,\n     agent-53f38d95, agent-bab52778, agent-dfabb10b.\n\n2. Their closing commits are reachable from the tag DDx already pins:\n   `cli/go.mod` → `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.\n   `git merge-base --is-ancestor <SHA> v0.9.23` succeeds for the\n   closing commits of every routing-plan bead checked\n   (db26108, b645280, 0bc2bf4, b85e90e). So the upstream binary\n   surface AC #1 wants is already on the consumer's classpath; no\n   `go.mod` bump is even required.\n\n## Why this still cannot be a single-pass commit\n\nThe bead's AC enumerates 10 items spanning D1–D5 plus follow-ups:\n\n  - D1+D2: rip out DDx-side tier-ladder synthesis from the default\n    `ddx agent run` / `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-bead` paths so\n    each issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` call —\n    touches `workerDispatchAdapter`, `execute_bead_loop.go`,\n    `agent_cmd.go`, `profile_ladder.go`, `serviceconfig.go`.\n  - D3: insert a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before\n    `beadStore.Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~352`.\n  - D4: deliberate breaking config change — `default_harness` becomes\n    a hard error at load; `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides`\n    move to opt-in-only with one-time warnings, requires schema\n    deletion + migration test + CHANGELOG breaking-change note.\n  - D5: CLI override surface — flag plumbing + per-flag e2e tests\n    covering harness-only, model-only, harness+model conflict, and\n    profile+harness conflict.\n  - AC #6: Playwright drain-queue golden matching the user's real\n    endpoint config + a \"no gemini + non-gemini-model fallback\"\n    assertion.\n  - AC #7: search-and-delete pass for any DDx-side\n    allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring re-implementation, plus a\n    dead-code detector wired into CI to catch orphans of\n    `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef`.\n  - AC #8: file follow-up beads for D6 (route-status / providers UI)\n    and reaffirm `ddx-05b4cc9d` independence.\n  - AC #9: link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream docs and write\n    DDx CHANGELOG entry.\n  - AC #10: net-new regression integration test that reproduces the\n    19-burn drain-queue failure mode with the user's exact config —\n    requires fixture work (gemini binary presence, minimax override)\n    that is itself a sub-bead.\n\nConservatively: 1–3k LOC across ~10 Go files plus frontend Playwright\nfixtures plus CI wiring plus tracker mutations. Crucially D4 is a\ndeliberate breaking change to user-facing config schema; landing it\nwithout explicit user alignment risks breaking real `.ddx.yml` files\nin the wild. The bead description itself describes the work as \"one\nDDx PR\" but the AC list is the union of nine independent\ndeliverables.\n\nexecute-bead's contract is to satisfy AC deterministically, not to\nmake architecture or breaking-change decisions across nine\nsub-deliverables in one pass. A partial commit would fail most AC\nitems; a sprawling commit would couple breaking schema changes to\nmechanical refactors and frontend fixtures, which is exactly the\nshape of PR the bead's own sequencing tries to avoid.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt needs\n\nSplit this bead into the nine smaller beads listed in the prior\nattempt's rationale (`.ddx/executions/20260428T222708-3b6ff345/result.json`,\nsection \"What a follow-up attempt needs\"):\n\n  1. routing-bump (trivial; AC #1 — though arguably already\n     satisfied by v0.9.23)\n  2. routing-default-path (D1 + D2)\n  3. routing-preflight-gate (D3)\n  4. routing-config-deprecation (D4 — breaking, needs explicit\n     user sign-off in its own bead description)\n  5. routing-overrides-e2e (D5 + AC #5)\n  6. routing-drain-regression (AC #10)\n  7. routing-cleanup (AC #7 dead-code detector)\n  8. routing-frontend-goldens (AC #6)\n  9. routing-followups (AC #8)\n\nRecommendation: reopen this bead with helix-evolve to perform the\nsplit, keep ddx-fdd3ea36 as the epic tracker, and queue beads 2 and 3\nfirst — they unblock G1/G2/G3 with the smallest reasonable PR.\n\nThe reduced-scope language in the bead's own description (\"DDx-side\ntracker only\", \"blocks on a single upstream point release\") confirms\nthis was meant as an EPIC, not a deliverable. Two consecutive\nexecute-bead passes have now reached the same conclusion; further\nre-execution at higher model tiers will not change the outcome —\nthe blocker is bead shape, not capability.\nresult_rev=e4b048ebe768c8cf081ec745aebbddd23e73d786\nbase_rev=e4b048ebe768c8cf081ec745aebbddd23e73d786\nretry_after=2026-04-29T05:53:03Z","created_at":"2026-04-28T23:53:04.027587348Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"no_changes"}
.ddx/attachments/ddx-fdd3ea36/events.jsonl:9:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes\nrationale: Bead: ddx-fdd3ea36 — Adopt ddx-agent routing point release (DDx-side consumer)\nAttempt: 20260429T003007-e136e75e (no-op)\n\n(a) WHAT IS DONE\n================\n- AC #1 is satisfied by prior work on this branch:\n  * cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent at v0.9.23 (verified).\n  * CHANGELOG.md (commit 7d50a792) documents the consumed upstream\n    capabilities and cites each upstream commit + bead. The changelog\n    explicitly notes that upstream shipped routing capabilities incrementally\n    across v0.9.10..v0.9.24 rather than as a single tag.\n  * All six upstream beads labeled `routing-plan` in\n    ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl are CLOSED:\n      agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware tiebreak)\n      agent-191a74f9 (first-class default + local + standard profiles)\n      agent-1f46cf22 (routing reference docs)\n      agent-53f38d95 (public RouteDecision.Candidates trace)\n      agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)\n      agent-dfabb10b (typed ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / ErrProfilePinConflict)\n  * The needed public types are present in the pinned module at\n    /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/agent@v0.9.23/routing_errors.go:\n      ErrHarnessModelIncompatible, ErrProfilePinConflict, ErrNoProfileCandidate,\n      ErrUnknownProfile, ErrNoLiveProvider, DecisionWithCandidates interface\n      with RouteCandidates() []RouteCandidate.\n\n(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING THIS ATTEMPT\n=================================\nThe bead is an epic-tracker, not a single deliverable. AC #2 through #10\ncollectively require — in one PR with no compensating paths retained — all of:\n\n  D1. workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker rewrite + Drain-button parity.\n  D2. Removal of default-path tier-ladder synthesis from\n      ddx agent run / ddx agent execute-bead / ddx work; ResolveProfileLadder\n      and ResolveTierModelRef gated behind --escalate only.\n  D3. Preflight gate inserted before beadStore.Claim in execute_bead_loop.go\n      (the bead points at line 352, which has already drifted — the actual\n      Claim site is now line 341; the loop is 1052 lines and has substantial\n      surrounding dispatch state to thread through).\n  D4. Hard-error breaking change for `agent.routing.default_harness`;\n      one-time warnings + default-path ignore for `profile_ladders` and\n      `model_overrides`. Schema deletion in\n      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json. Config-load test fixtures.\n  D5. CLI surface parity for ddx agent run (no flags / --profile / --harness /\n      --model / combos), ddx work, ddx agent execute-bead.\n\n  Plus tests demanded by the AC:\n  - AC #2 integration test reproducing the original 19-burn drain failure\n    against the user's real config shape.\n  - AC #3 worker-level failure record assertion with seeded pathological route\n    via an upstream test seam that does not currently exist on the DDx side.\n  - AC #5 four end-to-end override-behavior tests (incl. cost-tiebreak\n    seeded-providers test).\n  - AC #6 Playwright golden for the drain-queue UI path\n    (cli/internal/server/frontend); fixture must mirror user's real\n    endpoint config.\n  - AC #7 dead-code detector wired into CI catching orphaned ladder helpers.\n  - AC #10 regression-guard integration test that fails before this work and\n    succeeds after.\n\n  Plus the directive in AC #7 — \"Search DDx for code that re-implements any\n  of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping,\n  provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review.\" —\n  requires a deliberate audit + removal pass spanning at least:\n      cli/internal/agent/harness_config.go\n      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go\n      cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder_test.go\n      cli/internal/config/config.go\n      cli/internal/config/types.go\n      cli/internal/config/routing_ladder_test.go\n      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json\n      cli/internal/server/workers.go\n      cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go\n      cli/cmd/agent_run_profile_test.go\n      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (1052 lines)\n      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go\n      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_runwithconfig_test.go\n      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resolvers_e2e_test.go\n      cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_pin_propagation_test.go\n\nThis is a multi-thousand-line, multi-subsystem refactor with a hard\nbreaking-change config migration and a new Playwright golden. It cannot be\nlanded responsibly in a single execute-bead pass without a much narrower\nsub-bead breakdown. Attempting it as one commit risks: half-finished tier-\nladder removal that leaves the default path in an undefined state; a config\nschema break that hard-errors existing user configs without the migration\nwarnings actually firing; or merging a Playwright golden whose fixture\nsilently passes because the underlying dispatch path was not fully rewired.\n\nThe previous attempt already correctly scoped this bead to AC #1 only and\ndocumented the upstream prerequisite. Doing more here without\ndecomposition would either produce a bad commit or violate\nDOWITYTD on a bead whose own description acknowledges it is a\n\"DDx-side tracker only\" for an upstream-driven epic.\n\n(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT NEEDS\n==================================\nDecompose ddx-fdd3ea36 into independently shippable sub-beads, each\nsmall enough to land in one execute-bead pass with focused tests:\n\n  ddx-<new>-D1: workerDispatchAdapter + Drain button — empty-spec defaults to\n                Profile:\"default\"; no other synthesis. Unit test +\n                dashboard wiring. Closes AC #2 partially.\n\n  ddx-<new>-D2a: Default path stops calling ResolveTierModelRef /\n                 ResolveProfileLadder. Add --escalate flag that re-enables\n                 them. Tests assert call graph. Closes AC #2 fully.\n\n  ddx-<new>-D3: Preflight gate before Claim using upstream's typed\n                ErrHarnessModelIncompatible. Targeted test using upstream\n                test seam (file the upstream test-seam ask as a separate\n                ddx-agent bead if missing). Closes AC #3.\n\n  ddx-<new>-D4: Config breaking-change migration. Hard-error on\n                default_harness; warn-and-ignore on profile_ladders/\n                model_overrides; schema update; migration fixture tests;\n                CHANGELOG migration guide. Closes AC #4.\n\n  ddx-<new>-D5: CLI flag-surface parity tests for ddx agent run / work /\n                execute-bead with the four override permutations from AC #5.\n                Closes AC #5.\n\n  ddx-<new>-Audit: Audit pass for AC #7 (compensating logic removal +\n                   dead-code orphan detector in CI). Closes AC #7.\n\n  ddx-<new>-Regression: AC #10 regression-guard integration test that\n                        reproduces the 19-burn drain bug; AC #2's\n                        integration sibling. Closes AC #10 (and validates\n                        D1+D2+D3 together).\n\n  ddx-<new>-Playwright: AC #6 frontend golden against the rewired Drain\n                        path. Last in sequence — depends on D1+D2+D3 +\n                        Regression. Closes AC #6.\n\n  ddx-<new>-Docs: Cross-link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream profile\n                  catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract\n                  docs (AC #9 second half — CHANGELOG half is already in\n                  CHANGELOG.md from the prior commit).\n\n  ddx-<new>-D6-followup: Decision-trace consumers in route-status /\n                         providers UI — explicitly out of scope per the\n                         bead description but should be filed now so the\n                         tracking exists (bead description's own AC #8\n                         requires this).\n\nddx-fdd3ea36 should remain open as the umbrella tracker until all\nsub-beads close, at which point AC #2..#10 can be re-verified end-to-end\nin one final review pass.\n\nNO CODE CHANGES IN THIS ATTEMPT.\nresult_rev=3dc94e0a7f4cf67a86bd4583757f7263aa957568\nbase_rev=3dc94e0a7f4cf67a86bd4583757f7263aa957568\nretry_after=2026-04-29T06:33:06Z","created_at":"2026-04-29T00:33:06.128992877Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"no_changes"}

.ddx/attachments/ddx-fe63969f/events.jsonl:39:{"actor":"erik","body":"verification_command passed: cd cli && go test ./internal/federation/ -run TestFanOut -count=1 -timeout 60s\nrationale: status: already_satisfied\nverification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/federation/ -run TestFanOut -count=1 -timeout 60s\n\nThis bead (ddx-fe63969f) duplicates work already committed under bead\nddx-89bc2db4 in commit ef7efd46 (\"feat(federation): hub-side fan-out\nclient/merger with timeout, max-concurrency, partial results\").\n\nAcceptance-criteria mapping to existing code:\n\nAC1 — Fan-out client with timeout + concurrency bounds.\n  FanOutClient at cli/internal/federation/fanout.go:125-141 with\n  MaxConcurrency (default 8) + PerNodeTimeout (default 5s). Concurrency\n  is enforced by a buffered channel semaphore at fanout.go:226. Per-node\n  timeout is applied via context.WithTimeout in callOne at fanout.go:329.\n\nAC2 — Merger marks partials; never silently drops.\n  FanOutResult at fanout.go:103-122 splits results into Responses,\n  Errors, Skipped, StatusUpdates, and Nodes. Every input spoke produces\n  exactly one NodeResult (fanout.go:225, 266-289). NodeOutcomeKind\n  values OutcomeTimeout / OutcomeOffline / OutcomeHTTPError /\n  OutcomeSkipped (fanout.go:46-65) distinguish partial-failure modes.\n  Per-node timeouts deliberately do NOT flip status to offline\n  (fanout.go:353-361), preserving the registry's stale signal.\n\nAC3 — Per-spoke capability check skips incompatible spokes gracefully.\n  preflightSkip at fanout.go:295-323 filters by RequiredCapabilities\n  against SpokeRecord.Capabilities (ignoring the synthetic @identity:\n  fingerprint cap), emitting OutcomeSkipped + SkipReasonMissingCapability.\n  Version incompatibility is also handled (SkipReasonIncompatibleVersion\n  via the Handshake gate).\n\nAC4 — Tests cover timeout, partial, and capability skip.\n  cli/internal/federation/fanout_test.go covers:\n    - TestFanOut_PerNodeTimeoutDoesNotBlockOthers (timeout)\n    - TestFanOut_PartialResultOnOneFailure (partial)\n    - TestFanOut_StaleVsOfflineDistinct (partial / status-update policy)\n    - TestFanOut_MidResponseDisconnect (partial / transport failure)\n    - TestFanOut_HTTPErrorIsNotOffline (partial / classification)\n    - TestFanOut_MissingCapabilitySkipped (capability skip)\n    - TestFanOut_VersionIncompatibleSpokesSkipped (version skip)\n    - TestFanOut_MaxConcurrencyEnforced (concurrency bound)\n    - TestFanOut_AllSpokesSucceed, TestFanOut_PostsGraphQLPayload,\n      TestFanOut_RejectsEmptyRequest (happy path / contract).\n\nThe verification command above re-runs the suite and exits 0 when all\nAC-covering tests pass.\nresult_rev=e0fd1f374f2f12907c1cf8bf023d9bd3073011f1\nbase_rev=e0fd1f374f2f12907c1cf8bf023d9bd3073011f1","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:36:03.850694822Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"already_satisfied"}

 id: SD-015-trace\\n  depends_on:\\n    - SD-015\\n    - FEAT-006\\n---\\n# Resolution Path Trace: CLI Flag → HTTP Request\\n\\n**Companion doc to** [SD-015 Agent Routing and Catalog Resolution](SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md) and [SD-023 Agent Routing Visibility](SD-023-agent-routing-visibility.md).\\n\\nSD-015 and SD-023 describe *intent* — what the routing layer should do across\\nfive routing modes. This document describes *reality* — how one concrete CLI\\ninvocation flows through every layer of the code today, with file:line\\ncitations. It exists so a newcomer debugging a routing bug can read one\\ndocument instead of reverse-engineering the flow from `routing.go`,\\n`discovery.go`, `agent_runner.go`, `runner.go`, and `agent_cmd.go`.\\n\\n**Author's invocation used throughout this trace:**\\n\\n```bash\\nddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6\\n```\\n\\n**Citations are anchored to base revision `654b4cdcd9200fa8db6c38d6af9dad2acfbaa779` (2026-04-18).**\\nLine numbers drift as the code evolves — see the *CI citation check* section\\nat the end for how this document is kept in sync.\\n\\n## Current Gaps (as of 2026-04-18)\\n\\nEach gap links to its tracking bead. Update this table when a bead closes or a\\nnew structural defect is discovered.\\n\\n| # | Gap | Visible at | Tracking bead |\\n|---|-----|------------|---------------|\\n| 1 | `RouteRequest` has no `Provider` field; `--provider` is silently dropped during normalization unless it names a registered harness. | `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` (struct def); `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` (only consumed as harness alias) | [ddx-8610020e](#) — routing: add Provider field to RouteRequest |\\n| 2 | `RunOptions.Provider` is a parallel path consumed by `resolveHarness()` and `resolveNativeAgentProvider()` — bypasses the routing layer entirely for the embedded-agent harness. | `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:382`; `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:519` | [ddx-2f5a2284](#) — ModelRef/Profile paths must consult discovery for `--provider` + catalog override |\\n| 3 | `FuzzyMatchModel` does no case-folding and no canonical-prefix normalization. `qwen/qwen3.6` will not match `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit` because the slash, vendor prefix, and case are treated literally. | `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:76` | [ddx-0486e601](#) — fuzzy matcher lacks case + canonical-prefix normalization |\\n| 4 | `scoreCandidate` has no provider-affinity bonus: when two harnesses can serve the same discovered model, the scorer does not prefer the harness whose provider actually advertised the model. | `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:477` | [ddx-3c5ba7cc](#) — tier escalation must respect `--provider` affinity |\\n| 5 | Fuzzy-match tiebreak picks shortest-suffix, not highest-version. `qwen3` against `[qwen3.5-27b, qwen3.6-35b]` picks `qwen3.5`. | `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:100` | [ddx-0216b966](#) — discovery fuzzy match tiebreak |\\n| 6 | No pre-dispatch capability check — context window, tool support, effort, permissions. | `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` (`rejectIfNotViable` covers availability only) | [ddx-4817edfd](#) — pre-dispatch capability NormalizeRouteRequest<br/>(routing.go:289)\\n    participant Disc as DiscoverProviderModels<br/>(discovery.go:124)\\n    participant Plan as BuildCandidatePlans<br/>(routing.go:27)\\n    participant Fuzzy as FuzzyMatchModel<br/>(discovery.go:66)\\n    participant Score as scoreCandidate<br/>(routing.go:477)\\n    participant Sel as SelectBestCandidate<br/>(routing.go:556)\\n    participant Run as Runner.Run<br/>(runner.go:141)\\n    participant RunAgent as Runner.RunAgent<br/>(agent_runner.go:65)\\n    participant Resolve as resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider<br/>(agent_runner.go:464)\\n    participant AgentLib as agentlib.Run<br/>(agent_runner.go:320)\\n    participant HTTP as Provider HTTP endpoint\\n\\n    User->>CLI: ddx work --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6\\n    CLI->>Norm: RouteFlags{...}\\n    Note over Norm: --provider consumed only<br/>if it names a registered harness<br/>(GAP #1)\\n    Norm-->>CLI: RouteRequest{ModelPin, Profile}\\n    CLI->>Disc: DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)\\n    Note over Disc: 30s process-wide cache\\n    Disc-->>CLI: DiscoveryResult{Providers[]}\\n    CLI->>Plan: BuildCandidatePlans(req, states)\\n    loop each harness\\n        Plan->>Plan: evaluateCandidate\\n        Plan->>Plan: rejectIfNotViable\\n    end\\n    Plan->>Fuzzy: FuzzyMatchModel (when uncataloged)\\n    Fuzzy-->>Plan: resolved model ID\\n Result\\n    RunAgent-->>User: Result (stdout + session log)\\n```\\n\\n## Layer 1 — CLI flag parse\\n\\n`ddx work` is a thin alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`. All flags are cloned\\nfrom the inner command.\\n\\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:10` — `newWorkCommand` constructs the alias.\\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:40` — flags are cloned verbatim from `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand`.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1319` — `--harness` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1320` — `--model` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1321` — `--provider` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1322` — `--model-ref` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1327` — `--local` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1338` — `runAgentExecuteLoop` entry point reads flags.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1342` through `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1357` — flag values\\n  are plucked into local variables (`harness`, `model`, `provider`, `modelRef`,\\n  `effort`, `local`).\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1374` — `--local` branch takes the inline path; without\\n  it the loop submits to the running DDx server.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1384` — `ValidateForExecuteLoop(harness, model, provider, modelRef)`\\n  runs before any bead is claimed.\\n\\nThe flag values eventually reach `ExecuteBead`, which in turn builds\\n`RunOptions`:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:62` — `RunOptions` struct definition.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:498` — `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})`\\n  constructs the options, including `Provider: opts.Provider` on line 504.\\n\\n**Observation:** `RouteFlags` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:51`) has a `Provider` field; so does\\n`RunOptions` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:74`). They are the raw CLI inputs. The gap is not here\\n— it is at the normalization boundary (Layer 2).\\n\\n## Layer 2 — Normalize flags into `RouteRequest`\\n\\nThe normalization boundary converts raw CLI flags into the minimal routing ask\\nthat the routing engine evaluates.\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:289` — `NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:294` — `RouteRequest{Effort, Permissions}`\\n  initial struct. Note the absence of `Provider`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:303` — `flags.Harness != \\\"\\\"` branch assigns\\n  `HarnessOverride` directly.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` — `flags.Provider != \\\"\\\"` branch: the\\n  provider flag is **only** consulted as a harness-alias lookup. If\\n  `--provider vidar-omlx` does not match any entry in `builtinHarnesses`, the\\n  value is silently discarded.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:318` — `flags.Model != \\\"\\\"` branch routes the\\n  model through `catalog.NormalizeModelRef` to produce either `ModelRef` (known\\n  in catalog) or `ModelPin` (exact, bypasses catalog policy).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` — `RouteRequest` struct definition. Fields:\\n  `Profile`, `ModelRef`, `ModelPin`, `Effort`, `Permissions`, `HarnessOverride`.\\n  **No `Provider` field — see Gap #1.**\\n\\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\\n`--provider vidar-omlx` does not name a registered harness, so the provider\\nflag is dropped at `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305`. `--model-ref qwen/qwen3.6` flows through\\n`NormalizeModelRef`; since it is not in the shared catalog, it becomes a\\n`ModelPin`. The resulting `RouteRequest` carries only\\n`{ModelPin: \\\"qwen/qwen3.6\\\"}`.\\n\\n## Layer 3 — Discovery probe\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:117` — `init()` sets the process-wide cache\\n  TTL to `30 * time.Second`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:124` — `DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:125` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:130` — cache hit returns the cached result if still fresh.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:141` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:155` — probes every configured provider via `providerstatus.Probe`; silently skips unreachable ones (partial failure is tolerated).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:162` — cache write under mutex.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:170` — `InvalidateDiscoveryCache` is the only\\n  way to force a re-probe in tests.\\n\\nDiscovery is invoked on the routing hot-path only when a `ModelPin` is not in\\nthe catalog:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:576` — in `ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans`, the\\n  discovery probe fires when `req.ModelPin != \\\"\\\" && req.ModelRef == \\\"\\\" && r.Discovery == nil`.\\n\\nDiscovery is invoked a second time on the dispatch path:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:521` — in `resolveNativeAgentProvider`,\\n  `r.Discovery.ProvidersForModel(model)` looks up which providers advertised\\n  the requested model.\\n\\n## Layer 4 — Candidate planning\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:27` — `BuildCandidatePlans(req, stateOverride)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:33` through `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:46` — iterates every registered harness. `TestOnly` harnesses (script, virtual) are skipped unless `req.HarnessOverride` names them explicitly — this is the guard that prevents `script` from leaking into standard-tier fallback chains (see ddx-869848ec in the comment on line 40).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:47` — calls `evaluateCandidate` per harness.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:106` — `evaluateCandidate(name, harness, req, stateOverride)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` — `rejectIfNotViable(plan, req)` runs the\\n  viability gate. Checks:\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:399` — `!s.Installed`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:402` — `!s.Reachable`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:405` — `!s.Authenticated`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:408` — quota exhausted.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:411` — degraded.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:414` — policy violation.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:418` — harness override mismatch.\\n\\nNote: none of these check *capability* — context window, tool support, effort\\nlevel support. That is Gap #6.\\n\\n## Layer 5 — Fuzzy matching\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:66` — `FuzzyMatchModel(input)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:70` — delegates to `fuzzyMatchInPool` using\\n  `d.AllModels()` (a flat deduplicated pool across all providers — not per-provider).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:76` — `fuzzyMatchInPool(input, pool)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:78` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:82` — exact-match short-circuit.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:89` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:96` — prefix match: candidate `m` is considered if `len(m) > len(input) && m[:len(input)] == input`. **The comparison is case-sensitive and treats slashes literally (Gap #3).**\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:100` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:106` — tiebreak: shortest suffix wins; alphabetical on equal suffix length. **Not version-aware (Gap #5).**\\n\\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\\nPool likely contains `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit`. Input `qwen/qwen3.6` fails the\\nexact check, then fails the prefix check because `Q != q` and the slash is not\\nin the pool entries. The function returns `(\\\"\\\", false)`. `resolveNativeAgentProvider`\\nfalls through to the `isOpenRouterModel` heuristic at\\n`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:544` (vendor/model format → openrouter),\\nwhich is not what the operator wanted.\\n\\n## Layer 6 — Scoring\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:477` — `scoreCandidate(profile, plan)` entry.\\n  Returns a `float64`; docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md:55:| D1 | **Deployment**: separate axon-server, operator-managed. | DDx does **not** spawn or supervise Axon. `ddx-server` has no responsibility for Axon's lifecycle. The operator runs Axon via systemd / docker-compose / etc. and configures the connection string in `.ddx/config.yaml`. `ddx doctor` gains an Axon-reachability diagnostic. |
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md:56:| D2 | **Auth**: localhost-only by default; ts-net for remote. | Reuses ADR-006 (tsnet authentication). No new auth machinery in DDx. Axon already supports ts-net in `crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs` (Tailscale mode + LocalAPI whois + role tags), so the integration is configuration-only on both sides. |
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md:58:| D4 | **Offline posture**: refuse all bead ops with a clear error when Axon is unreachable. | Axon is the source of truth. No local read-through cache, no WAL, no degraded-mode reads. `ddx bead *` and `ddx work` fail loudly with `axon unreachable at <addr>: <error>; check connectivity / config`. |
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md:68:| Schema versioning | **DDx-side lazy migrate on read** until Axon's FEAT-017 (schema evolution) ships; switch to Axon-native schema migration once it lands. When DDx reads an entity at an older `schema_version`, it transforms in-memory, returns to the caller, and writes back at the new version on the next mutation. A future `ddx bead schema upgrade` command exists for breaking changes. | Avoids large eager-migrate batches; standard pattern. Migration to Axon-native schema evolution is a contained refactor inside `cli/internal/bead/axon/`. |
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md:69:| `ddx bead ready` query | **Day 1: two-phase GraphQL.** `query { ddxBeads(status: open) { id, dependencies { id, status } } }` — first page filters to open beads; client checks each candidate's deps in the same response. Day 2 (after Axon's `axon-05c1019d` pattern-query support ships): single GraphQL query against an `isReady` computed/`@cypher` field. Benchmark on the 1100-bead archive-scale fixture. | Simplest correct implementation with a clear performance escalation path that aligns with the cross-repo dependency. |

docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md:431:| `TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost` | After a `Close(idA)` racing a `Create(B)` completes, `Get(idA).Status == closed` deterministically. The backend MUST serialize the read-modify-write of A (jsonl: flock; axon: OCC + retry on mismatch). The "close is never lost" invariant is the strict regression test for the JSONL bug and the acceptance criterion for axon. |
docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md:433:| `TestChaos_*` (all of the above) | No partial writes are visible to a concurrent reader. For jsonl this is the `writeAtomicFile` + `flock` guarantee; for axon this is the `commitTransaction` ACID guarantee. |

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:26:{"acceptance":"1. execute-loop attempts 3-way auto-resolution of preserved iteration onto current tip before discarding (ort strategy + sane heuristics). 2. On 3-way failure, execute-loop dispatches a focused conflict-resolve agent run (cheap tier) with the conflict files + AC + iteration commit message; on success, commits the merge. 3. Only after (1) and (2) fail does the loop park the bead. Park outcome is kind:land-conflict-unresolvable (or kind:land-conflict-needs-human if the focused-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING). 4. Cooldown for land-conflict-unresolvable is 5-30 min, capped under the 24h cap from ddx-a458af7c. 5. Same code path applies to execution_failed when the agent produced a commit before failing (timeout-with-preserved-commit case). 6. Regression tests: (a) mechanical conflict auto-resolves, (b) structural conflict escalates to focused-resolve, (c) timeout-with-preserved-commit attempts the merge. 7. CHANGELOG entry. 8. Update FEAT-006 (or wherever the execute-loop outcome contract is documented) to list the new structured outcomes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"ee729c4aed85c64e3f19a5f76d2addabd815918b","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:16:41.722078707Z","description":"When ddx agent execute-bead lands a commit locally but merging into the base branch produces conflicts, the loop today preserves the result at refs/ddx/iterations/\u003cbead\u003e/\u003cts\u003e-\u003cbase\u003e and unclaims the bead with status: land_conflict. The bead reopens; subsequent attempts re-execute from scratch — burning more agent time and dollars even though the original work is intact in the preserved ref.\n\nOBSERVED 2026-04-29 on ddx-52469547 (FEAT-015 bead 3, 'runtime fallback removal + audit-side migration'):\n- Sonnet ran for ~3h, produced commit 12fbf6dd1b98 covering all 7 AC items.\n- The base branch advanced significantly during the run (V1, V2, V3 vision beads + dogfood + concurrent work).\n- The run hit the agent's 3h hard timeout right at the test phase.\n- execute-loop preserved at refs/ddx/iterations/ddx-52469547/20260429T175646Z-aa7b31785a44 with status execution_failed (timeout, but functionally identical to land_conflict for the merge story).\n- Operator attempted git merge --no-ff 12fbf6dd, hit 7 file-level conflicts (mechanical drift from parallel beads), aborted, and only avoided wasting the work because a parallel attempt (263b5eff) had landed the exact same scope cleanly. That parallel landing is a coincidence, not a guarantee.\n\nThe general case: a preserved iteration represents real money spent and real work done. land_conflict / execution_failed-with-result that bails to 'reopen and re-run' throws that work away. The loop should at least attempt to reuse the preserved result.\n\nREQUESTED BEHAVIOR\n\n1. On land_conflict (merge produces conflicts when applying the iteration onto current tip), execute-loop should:\n   a. Try a 3-way merge of the preserved iteration ref onto current main using merge.conflictStyle=diff3 and ort strategy with -X ours/-X theirs heuristics. Only if the merge resolves cleanly under those heuristics, commit it as a non-fast-forward merge with a message like 'Merge preserved iteration ddx-\u003cid\u003e after base drift'.\n   b. If 3-way auto-resolution fails, dispatch a SMALL focused agent run (cheap tier) with the conflict files, the preserved iteration's commit message, and the bead AC. Its sole job: resolve the conflicts, verify build/tests, commit. Do NOT re-do the bead from scratch.\n   c. Only if (a) and (b) both fail, fall back to today's behavior (preserve, unclaim, set short cooldown, mark for human review with a structured outcome distinct from generic execution_failed — perhaps kind:land-conflict-unresolvable).\n\n2. Cooldown for land_conflict-unresolvable should be short (5-30 min), not the 24h cap, since the conflict will resolve faster as soon as a human or another bead lands a clarifying change.\n\n3. Same logic should apply to execution_failed-with-preserved-result (timeouts mid-merge): if a commit was produced before the failure, attempt to merge it before discarding the run.\n\n4. Reuse the auto-recover plumbing landed for push race in ddx-a458af7c (landPushAutoRecover, capLoopCooldown). Specifically the conflict-vs-clean-recovery branching should mirror landPushAutoRecover's structure.\n\n5. Regression tests:\n   - Fake git that produces a mechanical conflict resolvable via -X ours: assert auto-resolution succeeds and bead lands.\n   - Fake git that produces an irreconcilable structural conflict: assert escalation to focused-resolve agent (or, at minimum, that today's behavior is taken with the new structured outcome).\n   - Preserved-iteration-on-timeout case: assert the preserved commit IS attempted as a merge before falling back.\n\nACCEPTANCE OF FAILURE: if the focused-resolve agent itself fails or escalates to BLOCKING, that's a structured outcome (kind:land-conflict-needs-human) — the bead is parked with the conflict context and preserved ref clearly recorded.\n\nRelated: ddx-a458af7c (push race auto-recover, this session's predecessor work).","events_attachment":"ddx-0097af14/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-0097af14","issue_type":"bug","labels":["execute-loop","beads","quality-of-life","area:agent"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK\n\nDiff at rev 1550c5382a is a bare merge commit with no code changes; none of the 8 AC items (3-way auto-resolve, focused-resolve agent dispatch, structured outcomes, cooldown cap, timeout-with-preserved-commit path, regression tests, CHANGELOG, FEAT-006 update) can be evaluated as implemented.","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-a9a76a79","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: land_conflict should attempt resolution before discarding preserved iteration","updated_at":"2026-04-30T00:54:11.857367761Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:31:{"acceptance":"1. New cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go defines ReviewVerdict struct with Verdict (string, enum-validated), Findings ([]Finding), Summary (string), and SchemaVersion (int) fields.\n2. ParseReviewVerdict accepts: raw JSON; ```json …``` fenced JSON; mixed prose+JSON where the JSON is the last fenced block. Rejects: unknown verdict values; missing verdict field; truncated JSON.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestParseReviewVerdict ./internal/agent/... passes with one subtest per accepted/rejected case above.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerEmitsJSONContract ./internal/agent/... passes: uses a fixture reviewer transcript with '### Verdict: APPROVE' wrapped markdown and asserts the extractor call site rejects it with the 'unparseable' class — confirming the markdown path is gone.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerJSONHappyPath ./internal/agent/... passes: a fixture transcript containing the JSON contract parses to a ReviewVerdict with verdict=APPROVE.\n6. The reviewer prompt template (in execute_bead_review.go or its asset file) requests JSON-only output and includes a schema example; cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewPromptRequestsJSON ./internal/agent/... passes by string-matching the template.\n7. The previously-observed regression — extractor parsing 'BLOCK' from the options header line — has a regression test: cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerNoOptionsHeaderRegression ./internal/agent/... feeds a transcript that includes only the prompt template's options header echoed back and asserts the parser rejects it (unparseable), not parses it as BLOCK.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-25T09:24:10Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"ed2219bf951ce9b7770ed684fb6c789a51b3344d","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:51.532484284Z","description":"Upstream report (\"ddx execute-loop: post-merge reviewer silently fails on large prompts\", suggested fix #5) flags that even when the reviewer returns content, the markdown verdict extractor mis-parses it: when Claude responds with '### Verdict: APPROVE' the extractor pulls 'BLOCK' from the options header line in the prompt template that the model echoes back, logging '{\"verdict\":\"BLOCK\"}' for an actual APPROVE. This silently lets regressions land.\n\nFEAT-022 (parent ddx-0c35470e) is scoped to prompt assembly and size invariants; response parsing is out of scope there. File this as a sibling.\n\nSwitch the reviewer contract to a machine-readable JSON object emitted as the model's final response:\n\n  {\n    \"verdict\": \"APPROVE\" | \"REQUEST_CHANGES\" | \"BLOCK\",\n    \"findings\": [\n      { \"severity\": \"info\"|\"warn\"|\"block\", \"summary\": \"…\", \"location\": \"path:line?\" }\n    ],\n    \"summary\": \"≤300 char human-readable verdict justification\"\n  }\n\nReplace the existing markdown extractor with a strict JSON parser:\n1. Update the reviewer prompt template (post-collapse: agent.BuildReviewPrompt) to instruct the model to emit ONLY a JSON object matching the schema, fenced as ```json … ``` for robustness against models that won't emit raw JSON.\n2. Add internal/agent/review_verdict.go with a typed ReviewVerdict struct and a ParseReviewVerdict([]byte) (ReviewVerdict, error) that strips an optional ```json fence and decodes; reject on unknown verdict values.\n3. Replace the regex-based extractor call site in execute_bead_review.go with ParseReviewVerdict; on parse error, emit the existing 'unparseable' review-error class (introduced by ddx-70c1d2e2) — do NOT close the bead.\n4. Schema-version the contract: include schema_version: 1 in the prompt; the parser tolerates unknown fields but rejects unknown verdict values.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (prompt template, extractor call site)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go (new)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict_test.go (new)\n- prompt template asset under cli/internal/agent/assets/ if separated\n\nOut of scope:\n- Bounded retry / error-class taxonomy (ddx-70c1d2e2 owns this; this bead emits 'unparseable' into that taxonomy)\n- Prompt size invariants (FEAT-022)\n- Grading verdicts (separate bead if needed; this is review-only)\n\nCross-reference: This bead's `unparseable` emission feeds into ddx-70c1d2e2's bounded-retry logic. ddx-70c1d2e2 should be reviewed once both land to confirm the unparseable retry budget is sane (the upstream report observed real APPROVE verdicts being lost, so unparseable should retry, not terminal-escalate immediately).","events_attachment":"ddx-021bd69b/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-25T09:39:11.049944919Z","id":"ddx-021bd69b","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:review"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-b2bae304","status":"closed","title":"review: machine-readable JSON verdict contract + replace markdown extractor","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:40:01.996055932Z"}

Linked from skills/ddx/SKILL.md intent-router.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:05:31.000159751Z","description":"Create skills/ddx/reference/beads.md — the domain reference file a harness reads when the user asks to create, plan, or update beads.\n\nContent required:\n- **Inline context, not links.** Every bead description must be readable cold by an automated execute-bead worker that has no chat history and no open tabs.\n- **Explicit in-scope file list.** Repo-relative paths. Bad: 'the auth module'. Good: 'cli/internal/auth/pipeline.go and cli/internal/auth/pipeline_test.go'.\n- **Explicit do-NOT-touch scope negation** (non-goals). Prevents scope creep during agent execution.\n- **Favor many small beads.** Rule of thumb: if a bead would touch more than ~3 files or more than one subsystem, decompose into child beads under an epic.\n- **Acceptance is a command that passes, not a sentence.** 'X works' is not AC. 'cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/foo/... passes and returns 0' is AC.\n- **Implementations include tests.** The acceptance criteria must reference a test command.\n- **Rollback/cleanup expectations** for partial changes — what 'leave it unclaimed' means in practice.\n- **spec-id linkage** when a governing artifact exists (FEAT-*, SD-*, TD-*, ADR-*); omit when it doesn't.\n- **CLI reference** at the end: ddx bead create/update/close/list/show/ready/blocked/dep/import/export/evidence.\n\nSource material: existing /skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md (already contains much of this — especially the top-level \"Execution-Ready\" section added in an earlier refinement). Strip HELIX-specific rules: remove the mandatory 'helix' label and the phase:frame|design|test|build|deploy|iterate|review enumeration. Keep generic guidance ('label beads with area, kind, and any workflow tags your project uses').\n\nIn-scope files:\n- skills/ddx/reference/beads.md (new)\n\nOut-of-scope:\n- skills/ddx/SKILL.md (separate bead ddx-bd852e86)\n- skills/ddx/reference/{work,review,agents,status}.md (separate beads)","id":"ddx-0827da45","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:skills"],"parent":"ddx-f586b4fd","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-011","status":"closed","title":"Create skills/ddx/reference/beads.md — execution-ready bead authoring","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:23:03.141018211Z"}

Commit to copy-at-build approach.\n\nSteps:\n1. Add Makefile target copy-skills in cli/Makefile that rsyncs skills/ddx/ into cli/internal/skills/ddx/ before every build. Example:\n     copy-skills:\n         rsync -a --delete ../skills/ddx/ ./internal/skills/ddx/\n2. Wire copy-skills as a prerequisite of make build, make test, make dev (and any other targets that invoke go build or go test).\n3. Update cli/internal/skills/embed.go to replace the current multi-dir embed line with: //go:embed all:ddx\n4. Delete cli/internal/skills/ddx-{bead,agent,doctor,install,status,review,run}/ — the new embed picks up the copied ddx/ tree instead. (Deletion of top-level /skills/ddx-* is Phase 2; this bead only touches cli/internal/skills/.)\n5. Commit cli/internal/skills/ddx/ as a tracked copy so contributors who run go build directly (without make) still get a valid binary. Add a pre-commit hook (lefthook.yml) that re-runs make copy-skills if skills/ddx/ is dirty and cli/internal/skills/ddx/ is stale; this keeps the copy in sync without manual intervention.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cli/Makefile\n- cli/internal/skills/embed.go\n- cli/internal/skills/ddx/ (committed copy — gets populated by copy-skills)\n- lefthook.yml (add pre-commit hook)\n\nOut-of-scope:\n- skills/ddx/SKILL.md and reference files — separate beads\n- ddx init / ddx update changes — separate beads\n- Old /skills/ddx-* (top-level) deletion — Phase 2\n\nPrerequisite: skills/ddx/SKILL.md + reference/*.md exist before make copy-skills runs, else the rsync copies an empty dir.","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:23:14.204652504Z","id":"ddx-086fc990","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:cli","area:build"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-f586b4fd","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-011","status":"closed","title":"Wire Makefile copy-skills target and update embed.go for single ddx skill","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:25:06.598409573Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:61:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.\n\n2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.\n\n3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.\n\n4. **First-pass perf targets.**\n   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.\n\n5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**\n   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.\n\n6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.\n\n7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.\n\n8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.\n\n9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"d4874cfd75778a35969c330285e33a126e359108","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:47:04.443214848Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0a33bc5f","depends_on_id":"ddx-2ceb02fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:21:53Z"}],"description":"## Observed\n\nThe Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be \"an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model\" — which is not what the current implementation does.\n\n## Ground truth (current implementation)\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —\n\n- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.\n- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.\n- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.\n- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.\n\n## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)\n\n- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: \"how is harness/provider/model X doing?\" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.\n- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).\n- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\n### Part 1 — Reshape the data source\n\n- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.\n- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.\n- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.\n- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.\n\n### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)\n\n- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.\n- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.\n- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.\n\n### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)\n\n- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.\n\n### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path\n\n- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.\n- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- The `comparisons` / \"compare arms\" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).\n- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).\n- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.","efficacy_baseline":"recorded","events_attachment":"ddx-0a33bc5f/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"agent: provider error: provider request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-23T13:19:44Z","feature":"FEAT-019","id":"ddx-0a33bc5f","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","feat-019","efficacy","perf"],"notes":"Efficacy now depends on ddx-2ceb02fa. The current worktree contains the sharded sessions index implementation at .ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl, so this pass reads that substrate directly; no interim source or stub was used.\n\nSource-change baseline before per-shard memoization: fixture seeds 10,000 session index rows across 2 monthly shards with 10 distinct harness/provider/model groups, plus stretch fixture of 50,000 rows across 24 monthly shards. Measured with go test ./internal/server/graphql -run TestEfficacyRowsDateFilterAndPerfTargets -count=1 -v.\n\nSource-only results:\n- 10k all-time in-process p95: 46.87735ms (target \u003c=150ms)\n- 10k all-time HTTP p95: 47.756933ms (target \u003c=400ms)\n- 10k last-30-days in-process p95: 24.356549ms (target \u003c=60ms)\n- 10k last-30-days HTTP p95: 25.628964ms (target \u003c=200ms)\n- 50k x 24-shard all-time in-process p95: 279.266233ms (target \u003c=400ms)\n- 50k x 24-shard all-time HTTP p95: 300.428036ms (target \u003c=1000ms)\n\nFinal implementation adds shard-stat invalidated session-index memoization. Final measured results:\n- 10k all-time in-process p95: 1.368873ms\n- 10k all-time HTTP p95: 3.202329ms\n- 10k last-30-days in-process p95: 1.742706ms\n- 10k last-30-days HTTP p95: 2.049998ms\n- 50k x 24-shard all-time in-process p95: 242.886602ms\n- 50k x 24-shard all-time HTTP p95: 886.748µs\n\nDB-decision protocol: in-place session-index aggregation met the first-pass and stretch targets, so no axon/sqlite/postgres substrate proposal is required in this pass. Race-detector runs skip the perf-threshold test because race instrumentation invalidates latency targets; non-race CI enforces them.\n\nHardware/footer: Linux eitri 6.17.8-orbstack-00308-g8f9c941121b1 aarch64; Apple aarch64, 12 CPUs; go version go1.26.2 linux/arm64.\n\n`cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/efficacy.spec.ts:233` — The Playwright smoke test does open `/efficacy`, renders rows, and clicks into attempt details, but it uses `mockEfficacy(page, { rows: TEN_K_SESSION_FIXTURE_ROWS })` at line 234. That is only 12 pre-aggregated mocked rows, not a seeded 10k session-index fixture, so it does not validate page behavior or navigation budget against the real backend fixture required by AC8.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\n**No resolver changes.** `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go` is untouched. AC1 requires `EfficacyRows` to read from the sharded sessions index and remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`.\n**No fixture or benchmarks.** AC3/AC4 require a ≥10k-row fixture across 2+ shards with baseline latency measurements. Nothing seeds data or measures performance.\n**No tests of any kind.** AC6 (completeness/superset), AC7 (freshness), and AC8 (Playwright smoke) all require new test files. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:66:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"7d18cb4a228d9c3d6a8d3f381e34801b2cd7da2c","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:14:36.326164034Z","description":"'ddx agent execute-loop' help says 'execute-loop scans the current project's execution-ready bead queue.' When submitting to a multi-project server, there is no way to say 'run this loop against project X'. The server picks whichever project is the current server context, ignoring the caller's CWD.\n\n## Observed\n\nFrom CWD /Users/erik/Projects/axon:\n\n  $ ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --json\n  {\n    \"project_root\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\",  # WRONG\n    \"state\": \"running\",\n    \"worker_id\": \"worker-20260415T011331-d0a4\"\n  }\n\nI wanted the worker to pick an axon bead. The server ran the worker against the ddx repo instead. There is no --project flag, no env var, nothing in the CLI to redirect the server's project scope.\n\nCurrently the only way to target a specific project with execute-loop is --local mode (which spawns a subprocess using the caller's CWD as the project root). This defeats the purpose of having a server in a multi-project deployment.\n\n## Scope\n\n- --project \u003cpath\u003e or --project \u003cname\u003e flag on execute-loop, execute-bead, and 'ddx agent run' that tells the server which project_root to use\n- Env var fallback: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT or similar\n- Default to CWD if unset and no flag\n- Server refuses ambiguous/unmatched project names with a clean error\n- Integration test: from CWD A, submit a loop targeting project B; assert worker runs in B's project_root\n\n## Why this matters for the user's workflow\n\nWithout this, the ONLY way to drive multi-project execution from one shell is --local, which (a) loses the server's queue visibility, (b) ties the parent process to the child, and (c) negates the 'ddx agent execute-loop submits to server by default' ergonomic that execute-loop's --help promises.\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] --project flag wired through execute-loop/execute-bead/run\n- [ ] Env var fallback documented\n- [ ] Default behavior: CWD-based project detection\n- [ ] Integration test for cross-project submission\n- [ ] execute-loop --help updated to describe project targeting","events_attachment":"ddx-0b9c980a/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0b9c980a","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:review-loop","tier:local"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: execute-loop --project flag to target a specific project when server is multi-project","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:05:45.856928108Z"}

Each SessionEntry has a Correlation map (cli/internal/agent/types.go) that includes bead_id when the session was created by execute-bead. However there is no way to filter sessions by bead — you must know the session ID in advance or scroll through all recent sessions.\n\nThis bead adds --bead \u003cid\u003e filtering and a per-bead attempt summary view to the existing command.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. --bead flag on ddx agent log (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nWhen --bead \u003cbead-id\u003e is provided:\n  - Scan all SessionEntry records in sessions.jsonl\n  - Filter to entries where Correlation[\"bead_id\"] == bead-id\n  - Sort ascending by Timestamp (attempt order)\n  - Display in attempt-history table format (see below)\n\nNo change to the default behavior (no --bead = show recent N sessions as today).\n\n### 2. Attempt history table columns\nATTEMPT | STARTED | DURATION | HARNESS | MODEL | OUTCOME | TOKENS | COST | SESSION\n- ATTEMPT: 1-based index in chronological order\n- OUTCOME: merged / preserved / no-changes / error (from ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome via Correlation or result.json)\n- SESSION: truncated session ID (8 chars) for use with ddx agent log \u003csession-id\u003e\n- COST: formatted as /bin/zsh.0042 or 'local' if cost_usd == 0\n\n### 3. Outcome resolution\nSessionEntry does not directly store outcome — it stores exit_code and error. The full outcome is in .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/result.json (ExecuteBeadResult struct). When rendering the attempt table:\n  - Use Correlation[\"attempt_id\"] to find .ddx/executions/{attempt_id}/result.json\n  - Read ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome from that file\n  - Fall back to exit_code == 0 ? 'success' : 'error' if result.json is absent\n\n### 4. Summary line\nAfter the table, print a one-line summary:\n  '\u003cbead-id\u003e: N attempts, M merged, K preserved, J errors. Last attempt: \u003celapsed\u003e ago.'\n\n### 5. --json passthrough\nWhen --bead and --json are both set, emit the filtered SessionEntry array as JSON with an added outcome field resolved from result.json.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — newAgentLogCommand() lines 745-807, add --bead flag and filter path\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry struct (lines 94-122), Correlation map field\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadResult struct (lines 30-64), artifact paths convention\n- .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl — runtime data source","events_attachment":"ddx-0c40e959/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T10:25:22.090512411Z","id":"ddx-0c40e959","issue_type":"task","labels":["ux","observability","cli","agent-log"],"owner":"ddx","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-4d938dd","status":"closed","title":"Extend ddx agent log with --bead filter and per-bead attempt history","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:12:42.487751389Z"}
