.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:101:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer running `go test`, my test runs do not leave behind registered projects in my real `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`. When a machine has already been polluted, I can prune it with one command. The server sweep is aggressive enough that obvious test-dir pollution does not survive a restart.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Fix A — test isolation enforced by CI.**\n   - Every existing test that instantiates `ServerState` or boots a server helper is migrated to an isolated state dir. No test writes to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`.\n   - A lint test (Go or shell) parses `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` (and any other directories that wire up `ServerState`) and fails if it finds a construction that does not pass an explicit isolated state path or override `XDG_DATA_HOME` first. Test cases added for both the allowed-pattern and the disallowed-pattern.\n\n2. **Fix B — sweep recognizes test-dir pollution.**\n   - `ServerState.migrate` drops entries whose `Path` matches any of:\n     - prefix `/tmp/`, `/private/tmp/`, `/var/folders/`\n     - regex `/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*\\d+/` anywhere in the path\n   - Missing paths matching these patterns are dropped on first sweep (no 24h tombstone).\n   - Reachable paths matching these patterns are still dropped (they are never real projects — the test dir may exist transiently but is never the user's registered project).\n   - Unit test seeds a fixture state with one real project, one `/tmp/TestFoo123/001` missing, one `/tmp/TestBar456/002` still on disk, and one `/private/tmp/TestMac789/001`; asserts only the real project survives.\n\n3. **Fix C — pruning CLI.**\n   - `ddx server state prune [--dry-run] [--state \u003cpath\u003e]` applies the Fix B rules to an on-disk state file.\n   - Writes `server-state.json.bak-\u003cYYYYMMDDTHHMMSS\u003e` before overwriting.\n   - Prints summary: `Pruned X of Y entries (Z kept)`. Dry-run prints the summary without writing.\n   - Integration test uses a fixture state containing 10 real + 100 fake entries, runs the command, asserts 10 remain and a backup file with exactly 110 entries was written.\n\n4. **Perf amplifier — regression test.**\n   - A test asserts `GetBeadSnapshots` does **not** call `os.Stat` on any project already tombstoned or pattern-filtered. (Implementation detail: sweep on startup + skip tombstoned in the hot loop.)\n   - If Fix A+B are in place, a fresh `go test ./...` run followed by a server restart produces a state file with ≤ 20 projects on a typical dev machine. Asserted via a CI job that runs the tests, starts the server briefly, inspects the state file, and fails if test-dir patterns are present.\n\n5. **Backward compatibility.**\n   - The prune CLI is safe to run on an existing polluted state file. The sweep upgrade is safe on restart — no data loss for real projects.\n   - On upgrade, a note is logged: \"Pruned X phantom test-dir projects from state file.\"\n\n6. **Cross-reference.**\n   - Bead notes link to `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) as the two surfaces whose perf problems are amplified by this bug. Those beads' fixture sizing assumptions were correct for a clean state file; the operator experience will still feel slow on a polluted machine until Fix C is run.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"a101e215a0b34590951f51340d2f25c07ff1c5cc","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:44:13.806406362Z","description":"## Observed\n\n`~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json` on a developer machine contains **7,343 registered projects**. Composition:\n\n- **6,936 named \"001\"** — all are Go test temp dirs like `/tmp/TestAgentCapabilitiesCommandJSON1451829170/001`, `/tmp/TestAgentCheckSuccess3666443068/001`.\n- **344 named \"002\"** — same pattern in tests that use `002/`.\n- **~60 real projects** (ddx, helix, axon, plus per-branch worktrees).\n- Only 135 of the 6,936 are marked unreachable; the rest are \"alive\" because their `/tmp` dir still exists or because `LastSeen` is recent.\n- State file size: 1.8 MB.\n\n## Root cause\n\n1. Go tests that exercise the server (or anything that wires up a `ServerState`) call `RegisterProject` with a temp-dir path. The state file they write to is **not isolated** — it defaults to the shared XDG location (`serverAddrDir()` at `cli/internal/server/server.go:67`) unless the test explicitly overrides `XDG_DATA_HOME` or constructs state via the `tc.StateDir` injection path.\n2. There is no test-side deregistration on teardown. Temp dirs get cleaned up by `t.TempDir()`'s own finalizer, but the project entry in the production state file stays.\n3. The reachability sweep (`ServerState.migrate` at `cli/internal/server/state.go:85-148`) tombstones missing-path entries and drops them 24h later. With thousands of entries being added per test run, the rate of addition outpaces the 24h GC.\n\n## Perf amplifier (cross-cutting)\n\nBoth `GetBeadSnapshots` (`state_graphql.go:49-105`) and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-489`) iterate every registered project per request. With N=7343 that's ~7k `os.Stat` + `store.ReadAll` attempts per bead-list or sessions query, mostly against dead paths. This is part of why `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) feel slow in practice even before their own N·M bugs are considered.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nThree independently-landable fixes:\n\n### Fix A — Test isolation (highest leverage)\n\n- Audit every test that calls `ServerState` / `RegisterProject` / boots a server. Each one must point at an isolated state dir (`t.TempDir()` + `XDG_DATA_HOME` override, or direct `tc.StateDir` injection).\n- Add a linting test: parse `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` for constructions of `ServerState` or HTTP server test helpers and assert each uses an isolated state dir. Fail CI otherwise.\n- This is the real fix. The rest is damage mitigation.\n\n### Fix B — Smarter sweep (defense in depth)\n\n- Sweep: any path under `/tmp`, `/private/tmp`, `/var/folders/*/T/` (macOS test temp root), or matching `/tmp/Test*` should be treated as a **candidate test-dir project** and dropped immediately on first sweep that finds it missing — no 24h tombstone.\n- Sweep should also drop entries where `Path` contains any `Test[A-Z]\\w+\\d+` segment (the Go testing convention), regardless of whether the dir currently exists. These are never real projects.\n- Unit test: seed a state with mixed real + fake-test-dir entries, run sweep, assert only the real ones remain.\n\n### Fix C — Manual cleanup CLI\n\n- `ddx server state prune` — read state file, apply Fix B's rules, write backup to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json.bak-\u003ctimestamp\u003e`, write cleaned state, print summary (X dropped, Y kept).\n- Useful for recovery on machines that have already accumulated pollution — like the reporter's. Keep even after Fix A lands.\n- Dry-run flag prints the summary without writing.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Moving state to SQLite.\n- Per-user project management UI. (Follow-up if the projects list becomes a real operator surface.)\n- Renaming \"001\" away from a confusing default. The name field is just `filepath.Base(path)`; fixing Fix A makes this question moot.","events_attachment":"ddx-15f7ee0b/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model \"minimax/minimax-m2.7\" for harness \"gemini\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-020","foo":"bar","id":"ddx-15f7ee0b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["feat-020","feat-008","testing","hygiene","perf-amplifier"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n**AC 1 (Fix A):** No changes to any `*_test.go` files to isolate `ServerState` construction, and no lint test added.\n**AC 2 (Fix B):** No changes to `cli/internal/server/state.go` (`ServerState.migrate`) to add test-dir pattern matching or immediate-drop logic. Resolve conflicts before committing. (0.00 seconds): exit status 1\n\nBut:\n  1. Verified no file in the repo matches lefthooks own regex `^\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c\u003c[ \\t]|^=======$|^\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e\u003e[ \\t]|^\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|[ \\t]` — including .ddx/beads.jsonl and the staged execution evidence dir.\n  2. The parent execute-loop never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.\n\n## Observed\n\nRunning 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --local --harness agent --model qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):\n\n1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.\n\n2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:\n\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    ...\n\n3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.\n\n4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same harness/model and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.\n\n## Expected\n\n- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate\n- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after \u003c messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely\n- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin\n\n## Scope\n\n- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status\n- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn\n- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls\n- Add a watchdog at the execute-loop level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget\n\n## Evidence\n\n- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)\n- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master\n- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally\n- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic\n- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome\n- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)","events_attachment":"ddx-25a41f51/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T01:39:31.55993889Z","id":"ddx-25a41f51","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:stability"],"owner":"ddx","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-e80e780a","status":"closed","title":"bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop","updated_at":"2026-04-15T03:46:00.206978601Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:145:{"acceptance":"- [ ] Option A, B, or C selected and documented in an ADR or design note under the ddx repo\n- [ ] Implementation lands with migration path for existing .ddx/beads.jsonl files (no lost bead history)\n- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ workers on the same machine produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state\n- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ machines (via ddx server or shared remote) produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state\n- [ ] Claim races are resolved by exactly one winner; losers see a structured 'already_claimed' error\n- [ ] Crash recovery: a claimed bead whose worker died returns to unclaimed after the heartbeat timeout (existing mechanism continues to work)\n- [ ] Existing beads.jsonl format continues to work as a read-only snapshot for offline tooling (jq queries, etc.)\n- [ ] Unit + integration tests covering the concurrency scenarios above\n- [ ] Migration test: apply the upgrade to axon's current beads.jsonl (748+ beads on sindri side, 30+ new on eitri side) and verify no semantic loss","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:20:46.447948869Z","description":"Fourth child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. .ddx/beads.jsonl is a git-tracked mutable ledger. Multiple writers on different machines or in parallel worker processes produce divergent files that require manual textual merge. git merge can sometimes append-merge JSONL cleanly if each side touches disjoint lines, but when both sides touch the same bead (claim / close / add-event), the merge is semantically broken even when git reports it as resolved.\n\n## Evidence\n\nOn axon, eitri and sindri both wrote to beads.jsonl independently for 14 days. Local has 134 commits touching the tracker; origin has 336. When I tried to rebase my planning commit onto origin, git reported conflicts in .ddx/beads.jsonl — and these conflicts would have been 'resolved' by git's textual merge logic even when the semantic outcome is wrong (e.g. two machines claiming the same bead with different claimed-machine values).\n\nThe existing execute-loop help text says:\n\n    By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background\n    worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current\n    process.\n\nSo a ddx server EXISTS and is supposed to coordinate. But .ddx/beads.jsonl is still the ledger, which means the server is not the source of truth — it's just a submission surface. The git-tracked file is authoritative, and that's what's racing.\n\n## Options (pick one during implementation)\n\n**A. Server-authoritative tracker.** When the ddx server is running, it owns beads.jsonl. All reads and writes go through the server's API. The git-tracked file becomes a periodic snapshot for backup/offline use, not the source of truth. Workers on multiple machines talk to a shared server.\n\n**B. CRDT append-only log.** Replace the current representation with an append-only log keyed by (machine_id, bead_id, sequence, timestamp). Each writer appends to its own partition; readers compute the effective state by merging partitions with a conflict-resolution rule (last-writer-wins per (bead_id, field), or better). git textual merge of the partitioned log is always safe because writers never share lines.\n\n**C. Per-bead lock files with atomic rename.** Each bead mutation acquires a lock file (beads/\u003cid\u003e.lock), writes beads/\u003cid\u003e.json atomically via rename, releases the lock. The JSONL aggregate is regenerated from per-bead files. Git tracks per-bead files, not the aggregate. Safe across local parallelism but not across machines without network coordination.\n\n**Recommendation**: A is the cleanest if the ddx server is already deployed as authoritative infrastructure. B is the best fit for the current decentralized model. C is a stopgap that solves intra-machine parallelism but not multi-machine drift.\n\n## Scope note\n\nDo NOT conflate this bead with the fetch/push/rebase triad (ddx-bd42f640, ddx-83ec1430, ddx-6aa50e57). Those three fix the git-level divergence; this bead fixes the tracker-level divergence. Both are needed — the triad without tracker safety would still produce semantically-conflicting beads.jsonl merges even when the underlying git history is linear.\n\n## Test cases\n\n- Two workers on the same machine claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly\n- Two workers on different machines claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly (requires network coordination)\n- Two workers attempt to claim the same bead: one wins, the other sees 'already claimed' and picks a different one\n- Recovery from crash between claim and CloseWithEvidence: the bead returns to unclaimed after a heartbeat timeout\n- Tracker state is deterministic from any valid ordering of concurrent writes (CRDT option)","id":"ddx-2791dc4f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","execute-loop","tracker","concurrency"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-820c201","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: .ddx/beads.jsonl concurrent-write safety","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:45:05.880884507Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:160:{"acceptance":"AC1. ProviderTimeout is configurable per-project via .ddx/config.yaml under agent.routing or agent.endpoints — e.g. agent.endpoints[provider].request_timeout_seconds — and the current 15-minute hardcoded constant becomes a default that can be overridden upward (or removed entirely) per provider.\n\nAC2. ProviderTimeout is configurable per-tier via the model-overrides path: cheap tier on a known thinking model (qwen3.6, deepseek-r1, etc.) defaults to a longer cap (e.g. 60 min or unlimited). Cloud chat models keep the 15-min default.\n\nAC3. The idle-read timeout (DefaultProviderIdleReadTimeout = 5 min) is the primary stalled-TCP-socket defense; documentation in cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go and the user-facing config docs explain the relationship between idle-read and wall-clock-request timeouts and when each fires.\n\nAC4. When ProviderTimeout fires, the error message names the exact timeout setting and the path to override it (e.g. 'request_timeout=15m exceeded; configure agent.endpoints.\u003cname\u003e.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml').\n\nAC5. execute-loop / execute-bead --request-timeout DURATION CLI flag exists for one-off overrides during debugging.\n\nAC6. Default for known-thinking-model surfaces (provider.type=lmstudio AND model name matches qwen3.6/qwen-r1/deepseek-r1 etc., or some other declared 'thinking' attribute) is bumped to a sensible value (e.g. 60 min) without requiring per-project config.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-29T20:58:18Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"932496","closing_commit_sha":"99375eaf330ea13838d40d2176d193ae3cd3453b","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:50:46.475563514Z","description":"Source: cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:30\n  const DefaultProviderRequestTimeout = 15 * time.Minute\n\nThis is hardcoded, not surfaced through .ddx/config.yaml or any flag, and is applied uniformly to every provider regardless of model class. It is set on every service path:\n  - cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:116  ProviderTimeout: DefaultProviderRequestTimeout\n  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:153           ProviderTimeout: DefaultProviderRequestTimeout\n\nThe comment justifies it as 'Defeats RC4 of ddx-0a651925: a stalled TCP socket that has delivered headers but stopped emitting body bytes would otherwise pin a goroutine until the outer wall-clock (3h) frees it.'\n\nBut that scenario is already handled by a separate idle-read timeout immediately below, on line 34:\n  const DefaultProviderIdleReadTimeout = 5 * time.Minute\n\nThe 5-minute idle-read timeout is exactly the right tool for the stalled-TCP-socket case (no body bytes for 5 min → fail). The 15-minute wall-clock cap on a single request is the WRONG tool — it kills any legitimately long single Chat call regardless of streaming health.\n\nConcrete impact observed today (2026-04-29 axon repo):\n\n1. axon-db7a6d0a worker (qwen3.6-35b-a3b on bragi via lmstudio)\n   - status: no_changes\n   - duration_ms: 1,762,081 (29 min total — one timeout + retry)\n   - log: 'WARN provider error, retrying attempt=1 err=\"provider request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s\" delay=1s'\n   - Retry consumed another full 15 min, also timed out, no commits produced.\n\n2. axon-80979cb8 + axon-db7a6d0a + axon-ab2e52e0 (3-worker cascade re-launch)\n   - All 3 hit 'provider request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s' on the first attempt\n   - User killed the run before retries could waste another 15 min × 3 each\n\nWhy thinking models hit this:\nqwen3.6-35b-a3b and similar 'thinking' / chain-of-thought reasoning models can spend 5–10+ minutes generating reasoning tokens before producing the first body delta the harness considers progress. Streaming output IS happening (so the idle-read timeout would NOT fire), but the harness is counting wall-clock against the entire request. A single hard problem can blow the 15-minute cap legitimately.\n\nWhy arbitrary timeouts are not helping:\n\na) Same value for cloud providers (claude, codex) and local providers (lmstudio, omlx). Local thinking models routinely need \u003e15 min on hard prompts; claude rarely does. Same cap is wrong for both classes.\n\nb) No way to opt out of the cap per provider, per model, per request, per project. /home/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/config.yaml has no knob.\n\nc) Retries inherit the same cap. A timeout-prone session retries with the same fate. Burns cycles and tokens.\n\nd) The cap defeats the cascade: when a cheap-tier qwen times out, escalation to standard claude is intended — but on a thinking model the timeout doesn't mean 'broken provider', it means 'this prompt is hard'. We then escalate after wasting 15 min, and the standard tier solves the problem in 2. Net effect of cheap-tier-first becomes negative.","events_attachment":"ddx-2c63bb95/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-29T21:15:18.844737462Z","id":"ddx-2c63bb95","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:routing","area:harness","kind:design-flaw"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-2db5279a","status":"closed","title":"agent: hardcoded 15-minute DefaultProviderRequestTimeout kills thinking-model requests; not configurable","updated_at":"2026-04-29T21:16:13.255743379Z"}

Update cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (execute-loop) and cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (execute-bead) to accept three NEW flags alongside the existing --harness and --model:\n\n- --provider \u003cname\u003e: passes through to ddx-agent as its `-provider` flag. Selects a named provider from   config (e.g. `vidar`, `vidar-coder`, `openrouter`).\n- --model-ref \u003cref\u003e: passes through as `-model-ref`. Resolves via the model catalog to a canonical target   (e.g. `code-high`, `code-medium`).\n- --model \u003cstring\u003e: already exists. Currently passed to ddx-agent as `-model` which does a direct model   override. THE FIX: when --model is given but no matching provider can serve it AND no catalog alias   matches AND no --provider/--model-ref is set, DDx must error BEFORE dispatching the agent — \"model 'X'   did not match any provider, catalog ref, or model-route; specify --provider or --model-ref explicitly\". Create a projectMiddleware (or helper function) that:\n   - Extracts {project} from the URL path\n   - Resolves it via GetProjectByID() or GetProjectByPath()\n   - Injects the resolved ProjectEntry into request context\n   - Returns 404 if project not found\n2. Register all document, bead, and doc-graph routes under /api/projects/{project}/...\n3. It still derives `escalationEnabled := spec.Harness == \"\" \u0026\u0026 spec.Model == \"\"` instead of mirroring the CLI's `escalate \u0026\u0026 harness == \"\" \u0026\u0026 model == \"\"`. That mismatch is why `ddx work` (default mode, server-submit) still hits \"all tiers exhausted — no viable provider\" with `model_overrides` pointing at non-served models, while `ddx work --local --no-adaptive-min-tier` succeeds. Verified live in this session: `worker-20260429T033325-bedf` failed; `--no-adaptive-min-tier` got `ddx-98e6e9ef` to merge at `115b47c0` on local-tier qwen for zero cost.\n\nSole child: `ddx-c7081f89`. Once that lands, this epic closes.\n\n## Strategic direction (preserved for future reference)\n\nDDx embeds the agent SDK as a Go library to produce traceable execution units: `(bead, input_state) → (output_state, processing_metadata)`. DDx owns the execution unit and its metadata (bead events, cost ledger, escalation summary, evidence directories), and it owns operator policy (`--harness`/`--model`/`--profile`/`--max-cost`/`--no-review`/`--escalate`). It does **not** need to own provider/harness/cost/local-first routing — agent SDK already does that.\n\n| Concern | DDx (now, post-87fb72c2) | Agent SDK |\n|---|---|---|\n| Profile selection | `RouteRequest.Profile` straight to agent | `default/cheap/standard/smart/local/offline/air-gapped/fast/code-smart/code-high` |\n| Per-tier model pinning | opt-in via `--override-model` | `RouteRequest.Model` |\n| Escalation on no-provider | opt-in via `--escalate` (CLI path); **not yet on server worker path** | `escalateProfileLadder` walks `ProfileEscalationLadder=[cheap,standard,smart]` on `ErrNoLiveProvider` |\n| Cost-aware ranking | n/a (delegated) | `internal/routing/score.go` ranks by `CostClass` (local=0 wins) |\n| Adaptive success-rate gating | `escalation.AdaptiveMinTier` (sticky 50-window cheap-tier lockout, only consumed via `--escalate`) | `routing.Inputs.HistoricalSuccess map[harness]float64` |\n| Decision trace | bead events `kind:tier-attempt` per RouteCandidate | `RouteDecision.Candidates []RouteCandidate` |\n\n## Optional follow-ups (not in this epic — file separately if/when needed)\n\nThese are no longer blockers; track separately if pursued:\n\n- **Default profile = `local`** so the operator-default honors the local-LLM-first goal without an explicit `--profile local`. Currently default is `default` which maps via project config.\n- **Inject `routing.Inputs.HistoricalSuccess` per-harness** to replace `escalation.AdaptiveMinTier` with agent-native success-rate routing. Frees DDx from maintaining its own success-window state machine.\n- **Delete `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go` helpers** once no caller (CLI or server) consumes them. Currently consumed only via `--escalate`; if `--escalate` is dropped or moved into agent, these can go.\n- **Delete `cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go::AdaptiveMinTier`** once the HistoricalSuccess injection above is in place.\n\n## Out of scope for this epic\n\n- Changes to agent SDK source — if a gap is found, file an upstream bead under `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.\n- UI/e2e work — the parent e2e drift epic `ddx-ccdf9cf9` covers that and is independent.\n- Server source changes outside `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — no API endpoint changes, no GraphQL schema changes.","events_attachment":"ddx-3f406266/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3f406266","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:routing","area:agent","kind:refactor","phase:build"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK\n\nDiff contains only execution evidence files (manifest.json, result.json) — no source changes to cli/internal/server/workers.go or anywhere else. None of the AC items can be verified from this diff.","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"routing-unify: delegate provider/harness/cost/local-first routing to agent SDK; ddx preserves execution-unit metadata + operator policy","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:30:06.928832371Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:229:{"acceptance":"- [ ] New command or subcommand: `ddx doctor divergence` or `ddx divergence check` prints the divergence report\n- [ ] Thresholds are configurable via .ddx/config.yaml (default: 5 commits each side, 7 days merge-base age)\n- [ ] execute-loop refuses to start in ERROR state unless --force passed\n- [ ] Structured event 'divergence_status' emitted at loop start and on every doctor run\n- [ ] Optional webhook integration via config.yaml alert_webhook_url\n- [ ] Unit tests: each state (clean, ahead, behind, diverged, network-down, drift)\n- [ ] Integration test: run the loop on a diverged repo and observe the refuse-to-start behavior\n- [ ] Docs: README section on divergence monitoring, thresholds, and how to recover","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:10.525089812Z","description":"Fifth child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. The 14-day silent divergence on axon (eitri local 134 commits ahead of merge-base, sindri/origin 336 commits ahead) was caught only because a human happened to try pushing. A trivial monitor running a few times a day would have flagged the problem at 5 commits, not 470.\n\n## Required behavior\n\nAdd a lightweight divergence check that runs either:\n- At the top of each execute-loop iteration (cheap, ~50ms), OR\n- Periodically via a scheduled task (ddx doctor or similar), OR\n- Both\n\nThe check computes:\n- `git fetch origin \u003cbranch\u003e` (tolerant of network failure)\n- local_ahead = `git rev-list --count origin/\u003cbranch\u003e..HEAD`\n- origin_ahead = `git rev-list --count HEAD..origin/\u003cbranch\u003e`\n- merge_base_age = `git log -1 --format=%ar $(git merge-base HEAD origin/\u003cbranch\u003e)`\n\nEmit a structured event 'divergence_status' with these fields. If:\n- local_ahead \u003e threshold (default 5): warn\n- origin_ahead \u003e threshold (default 5): warn\n- both \u003e 0: ERROR — diverged, neither side is a strict ancestor\n- merge_base_age \u003e threshold (default 7 days): warn (drift)\n\nIntegration surface:\n- `ddx doctor` prints the divergence report\n- `ddx agent execute-loop` refuses to start if in the ERROR state (both sides ahead) unless --force is passed\n- An optional webhook / notification channel (config: alert_webhook_url) for ops teams\n\n## Why this is P1 not P0\n\nThe fetch/push/rebase triad (P0) prevents NEW divergence. This bead catches EXISTING divergence and alerts before it grows. Without the P0 fixes, this alert would fire constantly; with them, it should fire rarely. Still valuable as defense in depth.\n\n## Test cases\n\n- Fresh clone: report is clean (0 ahead / 0 behind)\n- Local 3 commits ahead: report shows local_ahead=3, no warning\n- Local 6 commits ahead: report shows local_ahead=6, warning emitted\n- Both sides ahead: ERROR status, execute-loop refuses to start\n- Merge base 10 days old with both sides at 0: drift warning, execute-loop continues\n- Network unreachable: report shows 'unknown', no ERROR, execute-loop continues","id":"ddx-42dd982c","issue_type":"task","labels":["execute-loop","monitoring","observability"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-d16c6e3","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: divergence monitoring and alerting","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:56:12.030389438Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:258:{"acceptance":"The epic stays open indefinitely as a backlog for cost-tiered execution and measurement work. Closes ONLY if DDx pivots away from this optimization goal entirely (unlikely). Progress is measured by: (a) count of child beads closed that concretely improved the ladder, reviewed-rejection rate, or measurement granularity; (b) observable reduction in cost-per-closed-bead over time once the metric exists; (c) observable reduction in opus-review rejection rate as the cheap-tier work improves.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"c4518bd8b520a204440559044aeecaa799fcf414","created_at":"2026-04-15T03:47:42.980708133Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-4b59b816","depends_on_id":"ddx-e7254128","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:58:09Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-4b59b816","depends_on_id":"ddx-7bc0c8d5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:36:21Z"}],"description":"Standing optimization goal captured 2026-04-15: get the most work done for the least cost, and continuously improve our ability to measure and tune that trade-off.\n\n## Operating principles\n\n1. Cheap models do the work. Local (vidar-coder / qwen3-coder-next / other LM Studio hosts), then budget-tier cloud (claude haiku / gpt-5.4-mini), then standard (claude sonnet / gpt-5.4), then smart (claude opus / gpt-5.4 high-effort). Start cheap; escalate on failure.\n2. Strong models review. Every closed bead gets an opus review against its acceptance. Rejected reviews reopen with specific fixes.\n3. Deterministic fixes are the top rung of the ladder. When LLMs keep failing on a mechanical task, apply the fix manually; capture as input for routing policy.\n4. Measurement feeds optimization. Track per-bead which model attempted, how long, whether it passed review, what failure mode if any.\n\n## Existing bead tie-ins (helix review-loop epic from parallel agent)\n\n- ddx-a317208c exec: post-merge review agent on-by-default in execute-loop\n- ddx-dc231144 exec: tier-based auto-escalation driven by resource discovery\n- ddx-fdf031c9 exec: ddx bead review \u003cid\u003e — template a review prompt\n- ddx-44084b3e exec: ddx bead reopen \u003cid\u003e — atomic review-driven reopen\n- ddx-518b2690 exec: ddx agent run --output text — plain result extraction\n\n## Existing bead tie-ins (mine)\n\n- ddx-bd0691fe Epic: surface ddx-agent routing visibility (providers/models/check/route-status commands — prereq for routing policy)\n- ddx-bfc1aeaf Visibility 8: capture routing decisions in bead evidence (kind:routing) — feeds the measurement database\n\n## New child beads (to be filed under this epic)\n\nThe beads below are scope for this epic. File as children when we are ready to work each one. NOT pre-filed to avoid blocking the current Svelte+GraphQL migration.\n\n1. Per-bead cost aggregation: extend bead evidence schema to record tokens-in/tokens-out/cost-usd per attempt; add ddx bead metrics aggregate command that rolls up per-bead totals.\n2. Ladder success-rate analytics: ddx agent metrics tier-success — query last N closed beads, report success rate at each tier (vidar-coder, claude-sonnet, claude-opus, deterministic-fix).\n3. Prompt-context pre-filter: shrink the prompt for cheap-tier attempts — omit the full plan doc and include only the bead description + acceptance + 2 most relevant files.\n4. Failure-mode taxonomy: document the known failure modes (vidar long-context hang, read-loop stall, ship-unbuilt-code, opus-shape-miss, etc.) as bead evidence kinds so measurement can aggregate by mode.\n5. Review-rejection telemetry: aggregate opus-review outcomes (APPROVE / APPROVE_WITH_EDITS / REJECT) per originating tier so we know where the shape gaps are.\n6. Dashboard page for the metrics (requires Svelte migration Stage 4 to land first).\n\n## Why this is captured as an epic not a memory-only goal\n\nMemory is read; epics are tracked. Future sessions should see this in ddx bead list and be able to file child beads against it as we discover new measurement needs or optimization opportunities. The memory file is the principle; this epic is the backlog.","events_attachment":"ddx-4b59b816/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-4b59b816","issue_type":"epic","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:optimization","area:agent","area:cli","area:measurement","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:deferred","workstream:agent-upgrade"],"priority":2,"session_id":"eb-2dcc8231","status":"closed","title":"Epic: cost-tiered execution + measurement — maximize closed-bead throughput per dollar","updated_at":"2026-04-28T22:37:12.898039753Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:266:{"acceptance":"AC1. When an execute-bead session exits without committing in the worktree AND without writing .ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/no_changes_rationale.txt, the harness records a NEW dedicated status (e.g. 'agent_silent_exit' or 'no_evidence_produced') instead of letting ddx-land-coordinator land an iteration containing only execution evidence.\n\nAC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the harness catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.\n\nAC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.\n\nAC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the harness logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.\n\nAC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the agent-self-commit contract, the rationale-file fallback, and the new 'agent_silent_exit' classification.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25Z","claimed-machine":"sindri","claimed-pid":"3967690","closing_commit_sha":"cedf316fc6d5082eff11bff2f38ee5618958b20f","created_at":"2026-04-30T00:10:22.985451193Z","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.\n\nConcrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):\n\n  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)\n  Harness: claude / model: sonnet\n  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls\n  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit\n  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,\n    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts\n  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)\n  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of model time spent making them.\n\nThe same pattern appeared on:\n  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)\n  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)\n\nTotal burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.\n\nThe execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:\n  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e...'\n\nSo agent self-commit is the contract. But:\n\n(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the harness sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.\n\n(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.\n\nThe issue spans multiple harnesses (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one provider's commit semantics. Either:\n  - The harness should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration\n  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language\n  - The harness should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as harness-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25.451101407Z","id":"ddx-4e0a4a59","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:harness","area:execute-bead","kind:correctness"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","updated_at":"2026-04-30T12:43:46.370829758Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:269:{"acceptance":"On a host with a reachable lmstudio-protocol provider (e.g., bragi or vidar), running 'ddx work --local --once --harness lmstudio --model qwen3.5-27b' against any ready bead produces a result.json with duration_ms \u003e 0 and either exit_code=0 or a detail that names a real LLM-side failure (timeout, HTTP error, provider 5xx). 'exec: no command' with duration_ms=0 MUST NOT appear in any execute-loop result for a harness whose Surface=\"embedded-openai\". A Go test in cli/internal/agent/ covers this by constructing an lmstudio candidate with Binary=\"\" and asserts the dispatch picks the embedded-openai code path, not the exec-a-binary code path.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-17T22:49:09Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1521777","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:01:38.365525003Z","description":"Follow-on to ddx-1cfce460 (closed). The four recent routing fixes (55cbd50d, bb11d89d, 3598d014, bfcb7a61) resolved Symptom 1 (catalog→provider resolution). Symptom 2 (lmstudio candidate fails at exec) still reproduces in the installed binary (v0.6.0-alpha21 / commit 9436b58c built 2026-04-17T20:16Z).\n\nReproducer on host 'eitri' (bragi + vidar both healthy per 'ddx agent check'):\n1. Start 'ddx work' on a project with a ready bead and no pinned --harness/--model.\n2. Cheap-tier candidate is evaluated first.\n3. Routing event logs: {\"resolved_provider\":\"lmstudio\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen3.5-27b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}\n4. Tier-attempt event logs: tier=cheap harness=lmstudio model=qwen3.5-27b probe=ok, exec: no command\n5. result.json shows:\n   harness=lmstudio, model=qwen3.5-27b,\n   status=execution_failed, detail=\"exec: no command\",\n   duration_ms=0, exit_code=-1, failure_mode=unknown\n6. DDx escalates past standard (codex/gpt-5.4 also bails) and ends up at smart tier (claude-opus-4-6), which succeeds. Cheap tier burns \u003c1s but produces no inference and no useful diagnostic.\n\nRoot cause: registry.go:140-146 defines lmstudio with Binary=\"\", Surface=\"embedded-openai\", IsHTTPProvider=true. routing.go:202 has an explicit embedded-openai case for the lmstudio harness. But the exec dispatch layer is still treating lmstudio as an exec-a-binary harness, and with Binary=\"\" it produces the zero-duration \"exec: no command\" error instead of routing through the embedded-openai surface.\n\nEvidence preserved at:\n- /Users/erik/Projects/helix/.ddx/executions/20260417T214423-ed7adc95/{manifest,result}.json (cheap-tier attempt on helix-00e2486f)\n\nWhy this matters: every execute-loop run on this host silently wastes the cheap tier and escalates directly to the smart tier. The discovery work in 55cbd50d shipped on a routing-decision acceptance criterion without an execution-success acceptance, so the scoring layer correctly picks lmstudio but the dispatch layer cannot serve it — a hollow close on ddx-71fe194f's acceptance.\n\nRelated: the helix-side integration harness bead (helix-3fdba193) should add a cheap-tier-actually-executes assertion to prevent this class of regression from reappearing.","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:49:09.033895298Z","id":"ddx-501e87ef","issue_type":"bug","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:correctness","area:agent","area:routing","goal:throughput-per-dollar"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/internal/agent/registry.go","status":"closed","title":"lmstudio harness candidate routes correctly but fails at exec with \"exec: no command\"","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:52:30.037361567Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:275:{"acceptance":"- [ ] --output flag accepts text | json-result | session-jsonl\n- [ ] Default is text\n- [ ] Integration test covers all three modes\n- [ ] No behavior change for session-jsonl mode","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T05:26:10Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"cbaee23432d7fe8167b5dd1974fc237dc20437a4","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:48.719835899Z","description":"ddx agent run currently prints the agent's full session (JSONL events) to stdout regardless of flags. To extract just the final assistant text message, callers have to parse JSONL and walk the session log. This breaks trivial automation like 'review=$(ddx agent run ... --prompt @/tmp/r.md)'.\n\n## Scope\n- --output text: emit only the final assistant text message to stdout (no tool calls, no JSONL)\n- --output json-result: emit only the result-type JSON object (session metadata + final text)\n- --output session-jsonl: current behavior; keep as non-default opt-in\n- Default switches to --output text\n\n## Context\nObserved during axon ADR-018 loop: both review runs spilled 90-200KB of session JSONL to stdout, requiring python3 JSON-walking to extract the ~2KB opus verdict. --output text would have made the review invocation one line instead of three.","events_attachment":"ddx-518b2690/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T05:36:40.703736961Z","id":"ddx-518b2690","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:cli","kind:feature","gap:review-loop"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-dca40d97","status":"closed","title":"exec: ddx agent run --output text — plain result extraction","updated_at":"2026-04-15T05:36:54.864845558Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:280:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-30T03:39:31.542862754Z","description":"Finding from fresh-eyes review of the run/try/work boundary plan.\n\nProblem:\nThe plan says there should be one simple run command layered under try and work, but current governing docs and shipped skill references still describe ddx agent run, ddx agent execute-bead, execute-loop, quorum harness flags, and agent-provider command surfaces as active user/automation primitives. Examples found during review include FEAT-006 Agent Service overview/responsibilities, FEAT-010 Layer 1 wording, and cli/internal/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md persona/quorum/help examples. Parallel copied skill docs under .agents/skills/ddx and .claude/skills/ddx contain the same stale vocabulary.\n\nWhy this matters:\nThe docs are executable guidance for agents. Leaving legacy command names and flags in skills will keep agents routing work through the old surfaces even if the implementation adds top-level run/try/work.\n\nRequired fix:\nMake the public command taxonomy bright-line and uniform across feature docs, skill docs, and CLI help: ddx run is the single agent execution primitive, ddx try layers retry/review policy over run, and ddx work layers bead orchestration over try. Agent-internal service commands may exist only as hidden/internal plumbing and must not be documented as workflow commands.\n\nAcceptance:\n- FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 no longer describe ddx agent run/execute-bead/execute-loop as public orchestration commands.\n- DDx skill references in cli/internal/skills/ddx, .agents/skills/ddx, and .claude/skills/ddx use ddx run/try/work consistently.\n- Quorum/compare/persona examples are either migrated to the simplified surface or removed if they imply DDx harness routing.\n- A docs/skill grep test or validation step fails on stale public references to ddx agent run, execute-bead, execute-loop, --harnesses, or provider-routing examples outside explicit migration notes.","id":"ddx-52ce6249","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:docs","area:cli"],"parent":"ddx-deb9be7e","priority":1,"spec_id":"review:power-passthrough-boundary-stale-commands","status":"closed","title":"review finding: remove stale ddx agent run and execute-bead command guidance","updated_at":"2026-04-30T03:50:03.186901956Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:301:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:57:36.597150715Z","description":"'ddx agent doctor' currently reports a harness as OK if its binary exists on PATH. This is insufficient: for the embedded 'agent' harness, the actual backend is a remote inference server (LM Studio on bragi/vidar/grendel or a cloud API). A healthy binary + unreachable backend reports OK but cannot do any work.\n\n## Observed failure\n\nDuring the axon ADR-018 execute-loop, 'ddx agent doctor' reported '10/10 harnesses available' while bragi's LM Studio was stopped. Trying to run ddx-agent -provider bragi returned a 502 Bad Gateway. I had to manually curl each host to discover the real state.\n\n## Scope\n\n- Extend 'ddx agent doctor' to probe each provider in the agent config with a small 'models' or 'ping' request within a short timeout\n- Report per-provider status alongside per-harness status: provider name, host, last-probe result, last-probe ms, last-probe error\n- --quick mode: skip provider probes (current behavior), for fast checks\n- Default behavior: probe providers; show a two-column status (harness OK, provider reachable Y/N)\n- Integration with execute-loop's discovery-driven escalation (ddx-dc231144): the probe result feeds the per-provider cooldown map\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] doctor probes every provider in the agent config by default\n- [ ] Unreachable/5xx providers are reported as degraded, not OK\n- [ ] doctor output is machine-readable via --json\n- [ ] doctor --quick keeps the old fast-path behavior\n- [ ] Integration test: doctor against a fake provider that returns 502 reports the provider as unhealthy","events_attachment":"ddx-5bac3c6b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-5bac3c6b","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:review-loop"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: harness doctor must probe provider backends, not just binaries","updated_at":"2026-04-15T03:45:01.910986192Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:348:{"acceptance":"cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go ExecuteBead() contains ONLY: worktree setup (or accept pre-made worktree), prompt construction, agent harness dispatch, progress event emission, and a final completion event. No Merge, no UpdateRef, no preserveRef, no evaluateRequiredGates, no outcome classification beyond {task_succeeded, task_failed, task_no_changes}. All merge/preserve/gate logic moved to a new parent-side orchestrator that can be run interactively. Regression test: two workers on two different beads complete in arbitrary order; parent process merges both sequentially with interactive conflict resolution if needed; no worktree-internal merge code paths are exercised.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T05:36:33Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1412283","closing_commit_sha":"49c3d2bda576630dd2f6555bc143082344f079ba","created_at":"2026-04-14T04:02:25.941816183Z","description":"The current execute-bead worker does WAY too much: creates a worktree, runs the agent, evaluates required gates, decides merge-by-default vs preserve vs land-conflict, writes preserve refs, synthesizes commits on IsDirty, classifies 7+ outcomes, manages heartbeats, handles claim/unclaim contention, runs recoverOrphans on stale worktrees. This surface is why (a) two concurrent workers race on the main-branch merge and the loser's good work ends up under refs/ddx/iterations/ needing manual rescue, (b) no_changes gets misclassified as synthesized-commit success, (c) the gate-enforcement path and the merge path are entangled, and (d) the worker needs special privileges (update-ref, merge, rebase) that a simple task-runner should never have.\\n\\nThe correct design is the one the operator just described: execute-bead is a thin worker. 'I have been handed a worktree and a task. I will execute that task and make commits in the worktree. I will stream progress events to ddx server. I will notify ddx server when I am done, reporting the worktree branch and the commit(s) I made. Then I exit.' That's it.\\n\\nEverything else — merge to main, conflict resolution, required-gate evaluation, preserve-under-ref, decide whether to close the bead — moves to the PARENT process (the operator, or a driver tool running interactively in the terminal). Merge conflicts are resolved interactively the same as any other merge. A human (or an outer agent with full git authority) reviews the agent's commits and decides what to do with them.\\n\\nThis simplification deletes most of ExecuteBead() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go and moves:\\n- resolveBase / baseRev tracking → parent\\n- Merge() call → parent\\n- preserveRef / UpdateRef / refs/ddx/iterations/ writes → parent\\n- evaluateRequiredGates → parent (or a separate 'ddx agent check' step)\\n- outcome classification 'merged/preserved/land-conflict/error' → parent\\n- recoverOrphans → parent (cleanup runs when parent next launches workers)\\n- heartbeatIsStale reclaim logic → parent (parent tracks worker health)\\n\\nWhat the worker keeps:\\n- Take a worktree dir + task description (prompt) as input\\n- Launch the agent harness with the prompt\\n- Stream progress events to ddx server (session log + structured JSONL)\\n- On exit, emit a final event: worktree path, branch, commit shas (or empty), exit code, duration\\n- That's it\\n\\nExecute-loop likewise shrinks or goes away. In the clean design, a driver loop in the parent process picks ready beads, creates a /tmp worktree per bead, calls 'ddx agent execute-bead' (worker-only mode), waits for the worker's exit event from ddx server, reviews the result, and merges or discards. When two beads are in flight concurrently, the parent serializes the merge step — no race possible.","events_attachment":"ddx-679d2fc7/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T06:18:33.856661655Z","id":"ddx-679d2fc7","issue_type":"task","owner":"ddx","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-49c3d2b","status":"closed","title":"Simplify execute-bead worker: task-only, no merge/preserve/gate logic","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:12:51.136077845Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:360:{"acceptance":"- [ ] cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go Merge function is replaced (or supplemented) with a Rebase function that takes (dir, base, head, onto) and rebases head onto onto starting from base\n- [ ] On clean rebase, master fast-forwards to the rebased tip; one commit per bead\n- [ ] On conflict, commits are preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/\u003cbead\u003e/\u003ctimestamp\u003e/ via the existing iterationRef helper, the bead is unclaimed, the worker loop stops (same shape as the fetch-divergence path)\n- [ ] The 'chore: checkpoint before merge' commit is removed entirely; a dirty worktree at landing time is a hard error with a structured message\n- [ ] No code path in the execute-loop ever calls `git merge --no-edit` or any merge strategy — verified by grep\n- [ ] LAND-LOCK REQUIREMENT: while a rebase + ff-push sequence is in progress for a given projectRoot, no other rebase + ff-push sequence may run against the same projectRoot. The serialization point can be (a) a per-project flock on .git/ddx-land.lock, (b) a single goroutine in the ddx server process that owns all rebase-land operations for that project (preferred — this matches the meta-bead ddx-8746d8a6 coordinator design), or (c) an equivalent mutex held across fetch → rebase → ff-push. Without this, two workers whose rebases complete simultaneously will both try to advance master to different SHAs and one will lose. The test for this requirement: spawn N (N\u003e=5) concurrent land calls on the same projectRoot and assert that (i) exactly one commit is on master per submission after all calls return, (ii) the submissions are processed in some total order (not a set of parallel races), (iii) any submission that loses the race is either retried against the new tip OR preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/ with a clear reason — never silently dropped\n- [ ] Unit test: two sibling workers, second rebases onto first's tip, master has exactly 2 new commits in linear order with no merge parents\n- [ ] Unit test: conflicting rebase preserves commits and emits the land_conflict status\n- [ ] Unit test: dirty worktree at landing time errors out without producing a checkpoint commit\n- [ ] Integration test: running 5 parallel workers on the same machine for 10 iterations produces exactly 50 linear commits on master with no merge parents anywhere in the reflog\n\nThis bead is subsumed by the meta-bead ddx-8746d8a6 \"execute-loop: adopt human-PR landing model end-to-end\". The coordinator pattern in the meta-bead IS the implementation of this bead's rebase + land-lock requirements. If ddx-8746d8a6 lands first, close this bead as satisfied by the coordinator's rebase step. If this bead lands first, its implementation should be the foundation for the coordinator.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:20:11.501071514Z","description":"Third child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. When two workers run in parallel on the same machine (each in its own execute-bead worktree), and both complete and attempt to land, the orchestrator uses `git merge --no-edit` with the ort strategy to fold one worker's commits into master. This produces a merge commit tree instead of a linear history, and triggers repeated 'chore: checkpoint before merge' commits when the working tree isn't clean.\n\n## Evidence (source)\n\ncli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:59-69 defines the Merge function:\n\n    // Merge merges rev into dir's working tree via git merge. It commits any...\n    func Merge(dir, rev string) error {\n        _ = osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"commit\", \"-m\", \"chore: checkpoint before merge\").Run()\n        ...\n        out, err := osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"merge\", \"--no-edit\", rev).CombinedOutput()\n        if err != nil {\n            _ = osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"merge\", \"--abort\").Run()\n            return fmt.Errorf(\"merge: %s: %w\", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)\n        }\n    }\n\n## Evidence (symptom on axon)\n\ngit reflog master on axon's eitri machine shows 12+ `Merge made by the 'ort' strategy` entries on 2026-04-13 within a 2-hour window, each one merging a sibling SHA that never appeared on origin:\n\n    master@{8}  merge 5eb67fd0afab40ba4f9b06a8228760d69187af9d\n    master@{12} merge 77999324c59bb715ee6ef290255a5b31e17b1930\n    master@{13} merge 79772765312e3bc5a0715a6d9a2860016463e594\n    master@{17} merge 268f63283af2df6bfde94200b5159222ea4f996d\n    ... and nine more\n\nInterleaved with 'chore: checkpoint before merge' commits. This is the signature of two+ parallel workers each producing sibling branches that get merged back into master one at a time, each merge preceded by a defensive checkpoint.\n\n## Required behavior\n\nReplace merge-based landing with rebase-based landing:\n\n1. When a worker completes, its worktree is at commit W (child of base B)\n2. Between B and now, master may have advanced to M (due to sibling workers or fetch-from-origin)\n3. Instead of `git merge --no-edit W` on master, do:\n   a. `git rebase --onto M B W` in the worker's worktree (or equivalent plumbing via `git cherry-pick B..W` onto M)\n   b. On conflict: preserve the worker's commits under refs/ddx/iterations/\u003cbead\u003e/\u003ctimestamp\u003e/ (existing infrastructure at execute_bead_orchestrator.go:21-26), unclaim the bead, stop the worker loop\n   c. On clean rebase: fast-forward master to the rebased tip\n4. NO checkpoint commits. If the master worktree is dirty, that's a bug in the preceding workflow (should have been committed by the worker) — fail loudly, don't paper over with a checkpoint\n\n## Why rebase over merge\n\n- Linear history — no fan of merge parents from parallel runs\n- Conflicts surface earlier (during rebase, not as a semantically-broken merge)\n- Each bead produces exactly one commit on master; a clean log of 'this bead → this commit'\n- Push-ff-only from the sibling push bead (ddx-83ec1430) works because history stays linear\n\n## Interaction with fetch bead\n\nWith ddx-bd42f640 ensuring HEAD is current with origin before work starts, and this bead ensuring intra-machine landings are linear, a worker's commit sequence is always: fetch → linear base → work → rebase → push. No merges, no sibling fans.\n\n## Remove the checkpoint-before-merge pattern\n\nThe 'chore: checkpoint before merge' commits exist because the Merge function tries to handle a dirty worktree by committing it first. In the rebase model, a dirty worktree is illegal — the worker must have committed everything before the landing phase. If the worktree is dirty at landing time, error out immediately with a clear message about the worker not having committed cleanly. Do not silently checkpoint.","id":"ddx-6aa50e57","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","execute-loop","git","orchestration"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-43009cc","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: rebase sibling workers instead of git merge --no-edit (ort)","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:26:48.546705773Z"}

Linked from skills/ddx/SKILL.md intent-router.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:05:46.08980466Z","description":"Create skills/ddx/reference/work.md — the reference file a harness reads when the user says 'do work', 'drain the queue', 'run the next bead', or 'execute this'.\n\nContent required:\n- Default queue-drain surface: ddx work is the primary command (alias for ddx agent execute-loop). ddx agent execute-bead \u003cid\u003e is the primitive for targeted re-runs.\n- Pick-by-default, not by ID: ddx work picks the top ready bead. Override with --id only when debugging.\n- Verify independently before closing: run the acceptance criteria command yourself; do not trust the agent self-report. Claude/Codex agents hallucinate successful completions.\n- Close on success/unclaim on failure: close only on outcome 'success' or 'already_satisfied'; unclaim on every other outcome (no_changes, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed, execution_failed, structural_validation_failed). Never leave beads half-owned.\n- Testing expectations for bead implementations (not AC-level, but default guidance): favor stubs over mocks when a collaborator behavior matters to the test; exercise all layers where the project has them (unit / integration / e2e); measure coverage only where the project tracks it — do not introduce coverage tooling just for this.\n- CLI reference: ddx work, ddx agent execute-bead \u003cid\u003e, ddx agent execute-loop, with a note that ddx work wraps execute-loop.\n\nSource material: existing /skills/ddx-run/SKILL.md. Keep the lifecycle enforcement (claim → implement → verify → close). Strip TDD-as-mandatory framing if present (TDD is a methodology opinion; the ddx-native stance is 'tests must be included and must run', not a specific methodology).\n\nIn-scope files:\n- skills/ddx/reference/work.md (new)\n\nOut-of-scope:\n- skills/ddx/SKILL.md (separate bead ddx-bd852e86)\n- Other reference/*.md files (separate beads)\n- Testing persona work (Phase 3)","id":"ddx-6aa90265","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:skills"],"parent":"ddx-f586b4fd","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-011","status":"closed","title":"Create skills/ddx/reference/work.md — drain queue, execute-bead, verify+close","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:23:03.221075141Z"}

Most damaging of the three report findings because it hides incomplete work.\n\nObserved on axon `axon-c5cc071a` (RBAC field/row/entity spec, ~2000-word AC) 2026-04-19:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"id\": \"axon-c5cc071a\",\n  \"claimed-at\": \"2026-04-19T23:20:45Z\",\n  \"claimed-machine\": \"sindri\",\n  \"claimed-pid\": \"3479503\",\n  \"execute-loop-heartbeat-at\": \"2026-04-19T23:20:45.364638776Z\",\n  \"updated_at\": \"2026-04-19T23:25:18.98959532Z\",\n  \"status\": \"closed\",\n  \"events\": []\n}\n```\n\n4m33s from claim to closed, `events: []`, no execution bundle under `.ddx/executions/` referencing the bead, no git commit referencing the id, no branch, no notes. Reopened 2026-04-20 as `axon-c5cc071a`. Evidence this is worker-side: no other actor claimed the bead, `claimed-pid` matches execute-loop PID pattern, `execute-loop-heartbeat-at` was set at claim — a manual `ddx bead update` would not leave `events: []` while setting `status: closed`.\n\nRoot-cause hypothesis from report: the worker's cleanup path closes the bead on primary-task success even when the reviewer sub-step errors out without returning a parseable verdict (e.g. context-overflow `n_keep: 1,868,459 \u003e= n_ctx: 196,608` seen in reporter's LLM server logs, or any exception in the review state-machine path). A `bead.result` event that never gets emitted still leaves the bead closed if the exit path is not gated on verdict presence.\n\nClosure contract should be: a bead transitions to `status=closed` only when a valid terminal review verdict (APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK / SKIP) is recorded alongside an execution bundle and at least one bead event. Worker crash, malformed reviewer output, or context-overflow during review must transition the bead back to `in_progress` with a retry marker — never to `closed`.\n\nPrior related bead `ddx-b57be3ab` (closed) addressed BLOCK-with-empty-rationale but treated the rationale-missing case as `review_malfunction` without closing; this report shows a separate path where the closure itself happens despite reviewer failure.","id":"ddx-738edf47","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:exec","area:bead","kind:bug","kind:correctness","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:2","workstream:reviewer"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: bead closes without valid reviewer verdict — silent false-closure hides unfinished work","updated_at":"2026-04-20T16:23:03.738432327Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:399:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T06:24:35Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604f17d53ce26b5776472f9fa77a82a2eb1b9cba","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:35:08.058474818Z","description":"The ddx-bead skill guides bead creation but underspecifies what makes a bead *execution-ready* for automated execute-bead runs. Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop driving: beads that technically met the current skill's requirements (title, AC, labels, description pointing at spec-id) still produced bad execute-bead outcomes because the description lacked the context the agent needed to ship correct code in one pass.\n\n## Gaps in the current skill\n\n1. **Description quality is underspecified.** The skill gives AC quality rules but the description field just says 'per SD-004 Section 2' is OK. In practice, agents need:\n   - Exact file paths to touch (e.g., 'crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs')\n   - Cross-bead context when dependencies are listed (e.g., 'A1 landed the credential_revocations table in crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs — query it via SELECT 1')\n   - Inline spec excerpts, NOT just doc-id references. If the spec is ADR-018 §4's failure table, paste the table into the description. Don't say 'see ADR-018 §4'.\n   - Files NOT to touch (scope negation)\n\n2. **spec-id and the doc graph are unreliable for execution context.** The current skill implies spec-id linkage is sufficient. It is not — spec-id drives docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig() which only resolves HELIX-shaped doc references. Generic beads on non-HELIX projects have no equivalent. AND even when resolution works, the agent only gets a file path, not the relevant section. The bead description is the only place that carries content the agent can rely on.\n\n3. **No guidance on cross-bead context.** When bead B depends on bead A, B's description should describe what A landed (file paths, type names, key APIs) — not assume the agent will reopen A's history. Automated execute-bead does not have that context; each bead is read cold.\n\n4. **No guidance on verification commands in AC.** AC items like 'unit tests green' are too vague. Should be 'cargo test -p axon-server green' or 'bun test passes in sdk/typescript'. Named commands.\n\n5. **No guidance on bead size.** A bead should be one unit of testable work, sized so a single agent pass can complete it. Too small is overhead; too large defeats review. Heuristic: if the AC lists more than ~12 checkboxes, consider splitting.\n\n6. **No guidance on incorporating review findings on reopen.** When a bead is reopened after a REQUEST_CHANGES review, the author should either (a) fold the review findings into the description for the next attempt to see, or (b) rely on the tool to thread review events into the prompt (a separate ddx bead — see ddx-a317208c). The skill is silent on this.\n\n7. **No template for description.** A recommended description template (Scope / Files to touch / Cross-bead context / Verification / Quality bar) would make good beads cheaper to author.\n\n## Scope\n\nUpdate skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md to add:\n\n- A 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section that codifies the points above\n- A 'Description template' with recommended sections: Scope, Files to touch, Files NOT to touch, Cross-bead context, Verification commands, Spec excerpts inline\n- A 'Bead sizing' section with the ~12-AC heuristic\n- A 'Review-driven reopen' section that tells authors to update the description in place when a bead is reopened after a review rejection\n- A 'Common anti-patterns' section enumerating the mistakes observed during the axon execute-loop:\n  - Listing doc IDs without inlining the relevant text\n  - Not naming file paths in the description\n  - AC items that are not verifiable by a named command\n  - Depending on unmerged work without explaining what it landed\n\nKeep the existing required-fields and label-conventions sections — they are correct.\n\n## Context\n\nObserved during axon ADR-018 execute-loop:\n- Bead A1 was well-sized but initially had scope creep in its description (audit_retention_policies, which belonged to F1). Agent added the scope-creep table faithfully.\n- Bead A2's initial description said 'UserIdentity { provider, subject, user_id }' — a typo carried over from an earlier ADR revision. Agent implemented 'subject' faithfully even though ADR-018 §3 said 'external_id'. Test fails on current main and passes after the fix.\n3. `grep -E '\"id\":\"\\.execute-bead-wt-' .ddx/beads.jsonl` returns 0, OR returns 12 with a recorded decision in this bead's notes that the existing 12 are deferred to a separate repair bead.\n4. Its ID is referenced in this bead's events.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T00:56:04Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2278885","closing_commit_sha":"e1e5f7cfd8c9656260461a7e63d3e67883bdd77a","created_at":"2026-04-29T19:05:33.7239674Z","description":"## Two-layer problem\n\n### Layer 1 (mechanical bug)\n\nWhen a worker invokes `ddx bead create` from inside its execute-bead worktree, the resulting bead IDs are malformed. Reproduced today on this project:\n\n  /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx bead ready  → 11 entries with IDs like\n  `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1-e630aeae`\n\nThese were the read-coverage child beads spawned from the ddx-44236615 worker (commit ebeb2464). They have valid descriptions, AC, priorities, and labels — only the ID field is mangled. The pattern `.execute-bead-wt-\u003cparent\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003cshort\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e` matches the worktree directory name format used by `ddx agent execute-bead`. Most likely failure path: the bead-id resolver consults `os.Getwd()` (or equivalent) to derive a project context prefix, sees the worktree dir, and uses it instead of the real project root + a fresh `ddx-\u003c8hex\u003e` short hash.\n\nConcrete impact:\n- 50+ char IDs are unwieldy in CLI commands (`ddx bead show \u003cid\u003e`, `ddx bead dep add ...`).\n- Cross-project readers and the bead store assume `ddx-\u003c8hex\u003e` prefix matching for tooling (filtering, GraphQL adapters, MCP responses) — these long IDs may sort or filter unexpectedly.\n- The IDs encode ephemeral attempt timestamps, which leaks worker-internal state into a permanent identifier.\n- 12 such beads exist in the tracker as of this filing (grep `.execute-bead-wt-` in `.ddx/beads.jsonl`).\n\n### Layer 2 (design question — separate decision)\n\nEven if the IDs were correctly formed `ddx-\u003c8hex\u003e`, it is not obvious that `ddx bead create` from inside an execute-bead worker is the right pattern at all. Two alternatives the design has not articulated a position on:\n\n**(a) Append to the parent bead.** When the worker discovers sub-tasks, it appends them as structured items in the parent's description, notes, or a new `kind:discovered-subtask` event. The parent bead becomes the durable record of \"what this run found\"; future runs (or the operator) can decompose if/when the work warrants its own tracker entries.\n\n**(b) Surface via the execute-bead result.** The agent emits a structured `discovered_subtasks: [...]` field on result.json; the loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.\n\n**(c) Create new beads in-band (current behavior).** The worker calls `ddx bead create` directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue if the agent over-decomposes; complicates \"what was THIS run's contribution\" auditing.\n\nToday's behavior is (c) but undocumented. The 11 read-coverage children landing as a single agent's decomposition is exactly the failure mode (c) makes easy: one analysis bead spawned 11 implementation beads in a single run, all P0, all without operator review. The work may be valid — but the pattern needs a position.\n\n## In-scope outcomes\n\nThis bead's job is to surface both layers; the implementation work decomposes into follow-ups.\n\n1. **Concrete fix for layer 1**: bead-id resolver should normalize worktree paths to the real project root before allocating an ID. Test: `ddx bead create` invoked with cwd=`\u003crepo\u003e/.execute-bead-wt-...` produces `ddx-\u003c8hex\u003e`, not `.execute-bead-wt-...-\u003crandom\u003e`.\n2. **Mass repair for the 12 existing malformed beads**: either rewrite their IDs to fresh `ddx-\u003c8hex\u003e` (with a redirect or alias map for any external references), or close them as deferred and re-file with proper IDs. Decision deferred until layer 2 is settled.\n3. **Layer 2 decision**: file a separate design bead (or amendment to FEAT-006 / FEAT-010) that picks (a), (b), or (c) and documents the rationale. This bead does NOT pre-decide; it captures the question.\n\n## In-scope files (layer 1 fix)\n\n- `cli/cmd/bead_create.go` (or wherever bead-create resolves project context).\n- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` or `cli/internal/bead/id.go` if ID generation lives there.\n- A unit test that drops cwd into a worktree-shaped dir and asserts the resulting bead id matches `^ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}$`.\n\n(Locate via: `grep -rn \"execute-bead-wt\\|FindProjectRoot\\|projectRoot\" cli/internal/bead/ cli/cmd/bead_*.go`.)\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- The 11 read-coverage child beads' actual implementation work — that's separate beads.\n- Default behavior change for whether workers can/should create beads — that's the layer-2 design bead.\n- GraphQL or MCP changes to handle long IDs — fix the source instead.\n\n## Acceptance\n\nLayer-1 only (this bead):\n\n1. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/...` passes after the fix.\n2. New unit test in `cli/internal/bead/` (or `cli/cmd/`) that simulates a bead-create call from `cwd=\u003cproject\u003e/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-XXXXX-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003crandom\u003e` and asserts the new bead's `id` field matches `^ddx-[0-9a-f]{8}$`. Test fails before the fix, passes after.\n3. `grep -E '\"id\":\"\\.execute-bead-wt-' .ddx/beads.jsonl` returns either 0 (mass repair) or 12 with a recorded decision in this bead's notes that the existing 12 are deferred to a follow-up bead.\n4. A separate bead is filed (or referenced existing one updated) that captures the layer-2 design question (a/b/c). Reference its ID in this bead's events.\n\n## Why one bead, not an epic\n\nLayer 1 is a tight 1-3 file fix. Layer 2 is a design discussion that should be its own artifact, not a sibling task. Splitting them keeps each bounded.","events_attachment":"ddx-7eab13a6/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T00:59:34.510624284Z","id":"ddx-7eab13a6","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:bead","area:agent","kind:bug","kind:design"],"notes":"Layer-1 (ID resolver bug) fixed in this bead: detectPrefix() now takes a workingDir parameter and uses it for git commands instead of defaulting to cwd. This prevents linked worktrees from contaminating the prefix.\n\n22 existing malformed beads with .execute-bead-wt-* IDs are DEFERRED to a mass-repair follow-up. Decision pending on layer-2 design question (ddx-d3a4f89c) — once the in-band vs. append vs. result-field question is decided, the repair approach will be clearer. The malformed beads have valid descriptions, AC, priorities, and labels; only their IDs are wrong.\n\nFix in store.go correctly threads workingDir through detectPrefix and resolves the worktree-cwd contamination. However, the new test does not simulate the bug scenario: it never chdirs into a worktree-shaped path, so it would pass on main too — failing AC #2's 'fails-before-fix, passes-after' requirement. Layer-2 bead and deferral note are acceptable.","owner":"erik","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-22cc4abe","status":"closed","title":"bead-id resolver: malformed IDs when ddx bead create runs from inside an execute-bead worktree (+ flag whether workers should create beads in-band at all)","updated_at":"2026-04-30T01:01:23.445619428Z"}
