.ddx/executions/20260428T223637-9c85ab80/prompt.md:5:Standing optimization goal captured 2026-04-15: get the most work done for the least cost, and continuously improve our ability to measure and tune that trade-off.

## Operating principles

1. Cheap models do the work. Local (vidar-coder / qwen3-coder-next / other LM Studio hosts), then budget-tier cloud (claude haiku / gpt-5.4-mini), then standard (claude sonnet / gpt-5.4), then smart (claude opus / gpt-5.4 high-effort). Start cheap; escalate on failure.
2. Strong models review. Every closed bead gets an opus review against its acceptance. Rejected reviews reopen with specific fixes.
3. Deterministic fixes are the top rung of the ladder. When LLMs keep failing on a mechanical task, apply the fix manually; capture as input for routing policy.
4. Measurement feeds optimization. Track per-bead which model attempted, how long, whether it passed review, what failure mode if any.

## Existing bead tie-ins (helix review-loop epic from parallel agent)

- ddx-a317208c exec: post-merge review agent on-by-default in execute-loop
- ddx-dc231144 exec: tier-based auto-escalation driven by resource discovery
- ddx-fdf031c9 exec: ddx bead review <id> — template a review prompt
- ddx-44084b3e exec: ddx bead reopen <id> — atomic review-driven reopen
- ddx-518b2690 exec: ddx agent run --output text — plain result extraction

## Existing bead tie-ins (mine)

- ddx-bd0691fe Epic: surface ddx-agent routing visibility (providers/models/check/route-status commands — prereq for routing policy)
- ddx-bfc1aeaf Visibility 8: capture routing decisions in bead evidence (kind:routing) — feeds the measurement database

## New child beads (to be filed under this epic)

The beads below are scope for this epic. File as children when we are ready to work each one. NOT pre-filed to avoid blocking the current Svelte+GraphQL migration.

1. Per-bead cost aggregation: extend bead evidence schema to record tokens-in/tokens-out/cost-usd per attempt; add ddx bead metrics aggregate command that rolls up per-bead totals.
2. Ladder success-rate analytics: ddx agent metrics tier-success — query last N closed beads, report success rate at each tier (vidar-coder, claude-sonnet, claude-opus, deterministic-fix).
3. Prompt-context pre-filter: shrink the prompt for cheap-tier attempts — omit the full plan doc and include only the bead description + acceptance + 2 most relevant files.
4. Failure-mode taxonomy: document the known failure modes (vidar long-context hang, read-loop stall, ship-unbuilt-code, opus-shape-miss, etc.) as bead evidence kinds so measurement can aggregate by mode.
5. Review-rejection telemetry: aggregate opus-review outcomes (APPROVE / APPROVE_WITH_EDITS / REJECT) per originating tier so we know where the shape gaps are.
6. Dashboard page for the metrics (requires Svelte migration Stage 4 to land first).

## Why this is captured as an epic not a memory-only goal

Memory is read; epics are tracked. Future sessions should see this in ddx bead list and be able to file child beads against it as we discover new measurement needs or optimization opportunities. The memory file is the principle; this epic is the backlog.

.ddx/executions/20260426T113144-48b56189/prompt.md:85:+    "description": "Re-filed from agent-9465287e in /Users/erik/Projects/agent (closed there as 'tracked upstream'; the agent service is not the failure surface). Originally observed in ddx v0.6.2-alpha1 against agent v0.9.9 during SD-024 work.\n\n## Symptom\n\nWorker spawned with explicit pin:\n\n    ddx work --harness claude\n\nFirst attempt on a bead routes correctly to claude (e.g. opus-4.7) and produces a result. When the post-merge reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES the bead reopens. The next execute-loop iteration retries the bead but the worker's --harness override is NOT propagated to that retry. Tier-attempt events show:\n\n    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=standard harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found\n    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=smart    harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found\n    kind:escalation-summary winning_tier=exhausted\n\nNote harness= and model= empty — the retry constructs a service request with Harness=\"\". Smart-routing returns zero candidates at every tier above cheap (cheap suppressed by adaptive min-tier from prior failures). Bead bounces between REQUEST_CHANGES (cycle 1) → execution_failed (cycle 2 with no harness routing) repeatedly.\n\nConcrete instance: ddx-dd7fce7e in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/beads.jsonl shows the pattern at events around 2026-04-25T21:46-21:47Z.\n\n## Principle\n\n**Retries should use the same parameters as the initial work.** A reopened bead's retry must inherit every explicit pin (harness, model, model-ref, profile, provider) that the worker was spawned with. Otherwise the retry is a different request, not a retry — and the work cannot converge.\n\nThis is a general invariant, not specific to harness overrides. Any explicit pin the worker was launched with must propagate to every iteration on every bead the worker handles, including post-reopen retries.\n\n## Diagnosis (from agent-9465287e worker analysis)\n\nThe agent service surface (CONTRACT-003) is correct. The agent has no tier concept — tier classification and adaptive min-tier floors are ddx-side abstractions layered on top of the agent's per-call routing. Loosening the agent's routing engine to return candidates when the harness is empty would change behavior for every other caller and is not the right lever.\n\nThe fix is a few lines of option-propagation in the ddx execute-loop's reopen-retry path: when a bead reopens after REQUEST_CHANGES (or any review-driven reopen), the loop must copy the worker-level explicit pins into the next iteration's ExecuteBeadOptions / service.Execute request, the same way it does on the initial claim.\n\n## In-scope files\n\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or wherever the retry path constructs its execute-bead invocation)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go if review-driven reopens have a separate path\n- Regression test asserting the second service.Execute call carries the same Harness, Model, Profile values as the first when the bead was reopened by a reviewer\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Agent-side routing engine changes — the surface is correct\n- Adaptive min-tier behavior tuning (separate concern)\n- Tool-loop detection (separate concern, see agent-1bdb9b60 in agent repo)",

matches the same\n  pre-existing failure documented in commit 1718bdb5's message).\n\n## What is blocking\n\nThe bead's AC clauses\n  - \"field RunOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/service_run.go\"\n  - \"field RunOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go\"\ncollide with two enforced project gates that this bead's stated\nin-scope/out-of-scope split does not authorize me to break:\n\n1. SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint (cli/tools/lint/runtimelint, wired into\n   lefthook pre-commit at lefthook.yml:157). Pattern 2 of the analyzer\n   forbids any composite literal of RunOptions / CompareOptions /\n   QuorumOptions *anywhere* in staged Go code, and pattern 3 forbids\n   any function parameter typed as one of those legacy types in the\n   cli/internal/agent/ package. The lefthook block filters diagnostics\n   to staged file paths, so untouched files keep working but every\n   freshly staged file is checked in full. My prototype's new\n   compare_adapter_legacy.go and service_run_legacy.go contain\n   verbatim copies of the legacy entry points (RunViaService,\n   RunViaServiceWith, RunCompareViaService, RunCompareWithAgent,\n   RunQuorumViaService, RunQuorumWithAgent, RunBenchmarkViaService,\n   RunBenchmarkWithAgent, RunCompareWith, RunQuorumWith,\n   runCompareArmWith, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,\n   RunWithConfigViaService, runFixtureHarnessViaRunner, RunFunc, plus\n   the *Config wrappers that build CompareOptions/QuorumOptions\n   internally), and the runtime-lint gate fires on every one of them\n   (pattern-2 composite literals plus pattern-3 function params,\n   17 violations total). Touching session_index_test.go to redirect\n   TestProductionAgentExecutionPathsUseIndexedServiceWriter at the new\n   sibling files also re-stages the file and trips pattern-2 on the\n   pre-existing RunOptions{} literals at session_index_test.go:195 and\n   :278.\n\n2. The bead's own out-of-scope list. Eliminating the gate-violating\n   sites without moving them requires migrating the *callers* of the\n   public entry points off the legacy types — and those callers are\n   the explicitly out-of-scope packages:\n     - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (1 call to RunViaService at :1462) — the\n       bead lists \"cli/cmd/ migration\" as out-of-scope.\n     - cli/internal/server/server.go — listed as B22c (out-of-scope).\n     - cli/internal/exec/store.go — listed as B22b (out-of-scope).\n     - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (dispatchAgentRun at :927,\n       calls RunViaServiceWith at :938) and execute_bead_review.go\n       (dispatchReviewRun at :692, RunViaServiceWith at :704) — neither\n       file is in the bead's 10-file in-scope list, so per the\n       constraint \"Do not modify files outside the scope the bead\n       description names\" they are off-limits.\n     - cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go\n       (runAgentViaService at :72) and agent_runner.go (RunAgent at\n       :13) — both in scope per the description but their signatures\n       are called from out-of-scope runner.go (Runner.Run dispatches\n       to RunAgent at runner.go:110, runVirtualFn at :106, runScriptFn\n       at :114, runClaudeWithFallbackFn at :207, finalizeClaudeResult\n       at :209, recordRoutingOutcome at :231) and changing those\n       signatures cascades back into out-of-scope runner.go.\n\nThe two constraints are jointly unsatisfiable in a single attempt:\nremoving RunOptions from compare_adapter.go and service_run.go either\nrequires keeping the legacy entry points somewhere (and runtime-lint\nfires on whatever staged file holds them) or migrating all callers\n(and the bead forbids touching the out-of-scope files where most of\nthem live).\n\n## What a follow-up attempt would need\n\nA clean landing requires either:\n\na) **Bead resplit.** Split this bead into per-caller migration beads\n   that each cover one call site plus its package, so the runtime-lint\n   gate stays satisfied at every step. Concretely: one bead each for\n   (i) cmd/agent_cmd.go RunViaService → RunWithConfigViaService,\n   (ii) execute_bead.go dispatchAgentRun + RunViaServiceWith,\n   (iii) execute_bead_review.go dispatchReviewRun + RunViaServiceWith,\n   (iv) compare/quorum/benchmark callers (find sites first), and only\n   then a final bead that retires the now-unreachable RunOptions /\n   CompareOptions / QuorumOptions-typed entry points from\n   service_run.go and compare_adapter.go. Each step is independently\n   buildable, lint-clean, and AC-checkable.\n\nb) **In-scope list expansion.** Authorise this bead to touch the\n   currently out-of-scope cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go, execute_bead.go,\n   execute_bead_review.go, agent_runner_service.go, runner.go callers\n   (and the *_test.go files that still construct RunOptions{}), so the\n   migration can collapse the legacy entry points in one pass without\n   the runtime-lint gate firing on intermediate states.\n\nThe grade.go change in isolation — switching the lone\n`r.Run(RunOptions{Harness, Prompt})` call to\n`r.runInternal(RunArgs{Harness, Prompt})` via the dispatch seam from\nddx-dd640c18 — is clean (build green, runtime-lint green, tests green\nmodulo the pre-existing codex-CLI environment failure), but landing\njust that change would satisfy only 3 of the 7 AC items (the\nRunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions removals from grade.go) and\nleaves the AC items for service_run.go and compare_adapter.go\nunsatisfied, which per the bead's \"do not commit partial code\" rule is\nworse than a no_changes hand-off.\nresult_rev=12a2b57f9f71b9c3a70a4f3cc2c59bdca456f9df\nbase_rev=12a2b57f9f71b9c3a70a4f3cc2c59bdca456f9df\nretry_after=2026-04-27T01:59:32Z",

.ddx/executions/20260426T112631-fce6a923/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Re-filed from agent-9465287e in /Users/erik/Projects/agent (closed there as 'tracked upstream'; the agent service is not the failure surface). Originally observed in ddx v0.6.2-alpha1 against agent v0.9.9 during SD-024 work.\n\n## Symptom\n\nWorker spawned with explicit pin:\n\n    ddx work --harness claude\n\nFirst attempt on a bead routes correctly to claude (e.g. opus-4.7) and produces a result. When the post-merge reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES the bead reopens. The next execute-loop iteration retries the bead but the worker's --harness override is NOT propagated to that retry. Tier-attempt events show:\n\n    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=standard harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found\n    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=smart    harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found\n    kind:escalation-summary winning_tier=exhausted\n\nNote harness= and model= empty — the retry constructs a service request with Harness=\"\". Smart-routing returns zero candidates at every tier above cheap (cheap suppressed by adaptive min-tier from prior failures). Bead bounces between REQUEST_CHANGES (cycle 1) → execution_failed (cycle 2 with no harness routing) repeatedly.\n\nConcrete instance: ddx-dd7fce7e in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/beads.jsonl shows the pattern at events around 2026-04-25T21:46-21:47Z.\n\n## Principle\n\n**Retries should use the same parameters as the initial work.** A reopened bead's retry must inherit every explicit pin (harness, model, model-ref, profile, provider) that the worker was spawned with. Otherwise the retry is a different request, not a retry — and the work cannot converge.\n\nThis is a general invariant, not specific to harness overrides. Any explicit pin the worker was launched with must propagate to every iteration on every bead the worker handles, including post-reopen retries.\n\n## Diagnosis (from agent-9465287e worker analysis)\n\nThe agent service surface (CONTRACT-003) is correct. The agent has no tier concept — tier classification and adaptive min-tier floors are ddx-side abstractions layered on top of the agent's per-call routing. Loosening the agent's routing engine to return candidates when the harness is empty would change behavior for every other caller and is not the right lever.\n\nThe fix is a few lines of option-propagation in the ddx execute-loop's reopen-retry path: when a bead reopens after REQUEST_CHANGES (or any review-driven reopen), the loop must copy the worker-level explicit pins into the next iteration's ExecuteBeadOptions / service.Execute request, the same way it does on the initial claim.\n\n## In-scope files\n\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or wherever the retry path constructs its execute-bead invocation)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go if review-driven reopens have a separate path\n- Regression test asserting the second service.Execute call carries the same Harness, Model, Profile values as the first when the bead was reopened by a reviewer\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Agent-side routing engine changes — the surface is correct\n- Adaptive min-tier behavior tuning (separate concern)\n- Tool-loop detection (separate concern, see agent-1bdb9b60 in agent repo)",

.ddx/executions/20260426T112631-fce6a923/prompt.md:5:Re-filed from agent-9465287e in /Users/erik/Projects/agent (closed there as 'tracked upstream'; the agent service is not the failure surface). Originally observed in ddx v0.6.2-alpha1 against agent v0.9.9 during SD-024 work.

## Symptom

Worker spawned with explicit pin:

    ddx work --harness claude

First attempt on a bead routes correctly to claude (e.g. opus-4.7) and produces a result. When the post-merge reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES the bead reopens. The next execute-loop iteration retries the bead but the worker's --harness override is NOT propagated to that retry. Tier-attempt events show:

    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=standard harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found
    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=smart    harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found
    kind:escalation-summary winning_tier=exhausted

Note harness= and model= empty — the retry constructs a service request with Harness="". Smart-routing returns zero candidates at every tier above cheap (cheap suppressed by adaptive min-tier from prior failures). Bead bounces between REQUEST_CHANGES (cycle 1) → execution_failed (cycle 2 with no harness routing) repeatedly.

Concrete instance: ddx-dd7fce7e in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/beads.jsonl shows the pattern at events around 2026-04-25T21:46-21:47Z.

## Principle

**Retries should use the same parameters as the initial work.** A reopened bead's retry must inherit every explicit pin (harness, model, model-ref, profile, provider) that the worker was spawned with. Otherwise the retry is a different request, not a retry — and the work cannot converge.

This is a general invariant, not specific to harness overrides. Any explicit pin the worker was launched with must propagate to every iteration on every bead the worker handles, including post-reopen retries.

## Diagnosis (from agent-9465287e worker analysis)

The agent service surface (CONTRACT-003) is correct. The agent has no tier concept — tier classification and adaptive min-tier floors are ddx-side abstractions layered on top of the agent's per-call routing. Loosening the agent's routing engine to return candidates when the harness is empty would change behavior for every other caller and is not the right lever.

The fix is a few lines of option-propagation in the ddx execute-loop's reopen-retry path: when a bead reopens after REQUEST_CHANGES (or any review-driven reopen), the loop must copy the worker-level explicit pins into the next iteration's ExecuteBeadOptions / service.Execute request, the same way it does on the initial claim.

## In-scope files

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or wherever the retry path constructs its execute-bead invocation)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go if review-driven reopens have a separate path
- Regression test asserting the second service.Execute call carries the same Harness, Model, Profile values as the first when the bead was reopened by a reviewer

## Out of scope

- Agent-side routing engine changes — the surface is correct
- Adaptive min-tier behavior tuning (separate concern)
- Tool-loop detection (separate concern, see agent-1bdb9b60 in agent repo)

When the post-merge reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES the bead reopens. The next execute-loop iteration retries the bead but the worker's --harness override is NOT propagated to that retry. Tier-attempt events show:\n\n    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=standard harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found\n    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=smart    harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found\n    kind:escalation-summary winning_tier=exhausted\n\nNote harness= and model= empty — the retry constructs a service request with Harness=\"\". Smart-routing returns zero candidates at every tier above cheap (cheap suppressed by adaptive min-tier from prior failures). Bead bounces between REQUEST_CHANGES (cycle 1) → execution_failed (cycle 2 with no harness routing) repeatedly.\n\nConcrete instance: ddx-dd7fce7e in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/beads.jsonl shows the pattern at events around 2026-04-25T21:46-21:47Z.\n\n## Principle\n\n**Retries should use the same parameters as the initial work.** A reopened bead's retry must inherit every explicit pin (harness, model, model-ref, profile, provider) that the worker was spawned with. Otherwise the retry is a different request, not a retry — and the work cannot converge.\n\nThis is a general invariant, not specific to harness overrides. Any explicit pin the worker was launched with must propagate to every iteration on every bead the worker handles, including post-reopen retries.\n\n## Diagnosis (from agent-9465287e worker analysis)\n\nThe agent service surface (CONTRACT-003) is correct. The agent has no tier concept — tier classification and adaptive min-tier floors are ddx-side abstractions layered on top of the agent's per-call routing. Loosening the agent's routing engine to return candidates when the harness is empty would change behavior for every other caller and is not the right lever.\n\nThe fix is a few lines of option-propagation in the ddx execute-loop's reopen-retry path: when a bead reopens after REQUEST_CHANGES (or any review-driven reopen), the loop must copy the worker-level explicit pins into the next iteration's ExecuteBeadOptions / service.Execute request, the same way it does on the initial claim.\n\n## In-scope files\n\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or wherever the retry path constructs its execute-bead invocation)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go if review-driven reopens have a separate path\n- Regression test asserting the second service.Execute call carries the same Harness, Model, Profile values as the first when the bead was reopened by a reviewer\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Agent-side routing engine changes — the surface is correct\n- Adaptive min-tier behavior tuning (separate concern)\n- Tool-loop detection (separate concern, see agent-1bdb9b60 in agent repo)",
.ddx/executions/20260425T233452-527fd4e5/prompt.md:5:Re-filed from agent-9465287e in /Users/erik/Projects/agent (closed there as 'tracked upstream'; the agent service is not the failure surface). Originally observed in ddx v0.6.2-alpha1 against agent v0.9.9 during SD-024 work.

## Symptom

Worker spawned with explicit pin:

    ddx work --harness claude

First attempt on a bead routes correctly to claude (e.g. opus-4.7) and produces a result. When the post-merge reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES the bead reopens. The next execute-loop iteration retries the bead but the worker's --harness override is NOT propagated to that retry. Tier-attempt events show:

    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=standard harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found
    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=smart    harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found
    kind:escalation-summary winning_tier=exhausted

Note harness= and model= empty — the retry constructs a service request with Harness="". Smart-routing returns zero candidates at every tier above cheap (cheap suppressed by adaptive min-tier from prior failures). Bead bounces between REQUEST_CHANGES (cycle 1) → execution_failed (cycle 2 with no harness routing) repeatedly.

Concrete instance: ddx-dd7fce7e in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/beads.jsonl shows the pattern at events around 2026-04-25T21:46-21:47Z.

## Principle

**Retries should use the same parameters as the initial work.** A reopened bead's retry must inherit every explicit pin (harness, model, model-ref, profile, provider) that the worker was spawned with. Otherwise the retry is a different request, not a retry — and the work cannot converge.

This is a general invariant, not specific to harness overrides. Any explicit pin the worker was launched with must propagate to every iteration on every bead the worker handles, including post-reopen retries.

## Diagnosis (from agent-9465287e worker analysis)

The agent service surface (CONTRACT-003) is correct. The agent has no tier concept — tier classification and adaptive min-tier floors are ddx-side abstractions layered on top of the agent's per-call routing. Loosening the agent's routing engine to return candidates when the harness is empty would change behavior for every other caller and is not the right lever.

The fix is a few lines of option-propagation in the ddx execute-loop's reopen-retry path: when a bead reopens after REQUEST_CHANGES (or any review-driven reopen), the loop must copy the worker-level explicit pins into the next iteration's ExecuteBeadOptions / service.Execute request, the same way it does on the initial claim.

## In-scope files

- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or wherever the retry path constructs its execute-bead invocation)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go if review-driven reopens have a separate path
- Regression test asserting the second service.Execute call carries the same Harness, Model, Profile values as the first when the bead was reopened by a reviewer

## Out of scope

- Agent-side routing engine changes — the surface is correct
- Adaptive min-tier behavior tuning (separate concern)
- Tool-loop detection (separate concern, see agent-1bdb9b60 in agent repo)

cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go\n       (runAgentViaService at :72) and agent_runner.go (RunAgent at\n       :13) — both in scope per the description but their signatures\n       are called from out-of-scope runner.go (Runner.Run dispatches\n       to RunAgent at runner.go:110, runVirtualFn at :106, runScriptFn\n       at :114, runClaudeWithFallbackFn at :207, finalizeClaudeResult\n       at :209, recordRoutingOutcome at :231) and changing those\n       signatures cascades back into out-of-scope runner.go.\n\nThe two constraints are jointly unsatisfiable in a single attempt:\nremoving RunOptions from compare_adapter.go and service_run.go either\nrequires keeping the legacy entry points somewhere (and runtime-lint\nfires on whatever staged file holds them) or migrating all callers\n(and the bead forbids touching the out-of-scope files where most of\nthem live).\n\n## What a follow-up attempt would need\n\nA clean landing requires either:\n\na) **Bead resplit.** Split this bead into per-caller migration beads\n   that each cover one call site plus its package, so the runtime-lint\n   gate stays satisfied at every step. Loosening the agent's routing engine to return candidates when the harness is empty would change behavior for every other caller and is not the right lever.\n\nThe fix is a few lines of option-propagation in the ddx execute-loop's reopen-retry path: when a bead reopens after REQUEST_CHANGES (or any review-driven reopen), the loop must copy the worker-level explicit pins into the next iteration's ExecuteBeadOptions / service.Execute request, the same way it does on the initial claim.\n\n## In-scope files\n\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or wherever the retry path constructs its execute-bead invocation)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go if review-driven reopens have a separate path\n- Regression test asserting the second service.Execute call carries the same Harness, Model, Profile values as the first when the bead was reopened by a reviewer\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Agent-side routing engine changes — the surface is correct\n- Adaptive min-tier behavior tuning (separate concern)\n- Tool-loop detection (separate concern, see agent-1bdb9b60 in agent repo)",

.ddx/executions/20260429T065203-5c940767/manifest.json:45:          "body": "no_changes\nrationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)\n\n## Decision\n\nStopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a\nmulti-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it\nin one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that\nfails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config\nschema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs\nwithout the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.\n\n## What is already done (verified in this worktree)\n\n1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under\n   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in\n   `closed` status:\n     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)\n     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)\n     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)\n     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)\n     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)\n     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)\n\n2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**\n   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.\n   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns\n   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)\n   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1\n   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.\n\n## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass\n\n### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative\n\nAC #1 asks for \"a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class\ndefault and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +\ngating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost\ndimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback.\"\n\nReading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:\n  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.\n  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.\n  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,\n    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent\n    \"routing point release\" narrative.\n\nThe closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as\nside-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the\ncombined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires\neither (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a \"v0.9.25 — routing\npoint release\" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead\ndescription must be relaxed to accept \"the code is in v0.9.23, even if\nno single CHANGELOG block summarizes it.\" Either resolution is a\ngovernance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.\n\n### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)\n\nThese criteria collectively require:\n  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one\n    `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` call with no Model / no Harness /\n    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;\n  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim\n    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);\n  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,\n    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,\n    with one-time process warnings;\n  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to\n    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;\n  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /\n    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with\n    dead-code detection in CI;\n  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn\n    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override\n    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness\n    conflict);\n  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's\n    real config;\n  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.\n\nThis is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:\n  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`\n  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)\n  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)\n  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)\n  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)\n\nThat work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead\nexplicitly bundles into \"one DDx PR\" — but a PR of that size needs\narchitectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking\nconfig change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the\nfirst line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC\ndeterministically, not to make architecture decisions.\n\n### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads\n\nThe bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:\n  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,\n  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)\n    stays independent.\n\nFiling beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the\nproject's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is\nmechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.\n\n### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden\n\nA net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical\n19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test\n(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not\nhave gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt needs\n\nTo make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead\nmust be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:\n\n1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever\n   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add\n   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially\n   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.\n\n2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default\n   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:\n   \"default\")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.\n\n3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before\n   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded\n   incompatibility.\n\n4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on\n   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +\n   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.\n\n5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override\n   combination.\n\n6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test\n   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.\n\n7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of\n   compensating logic.\n\n8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.\n\n9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI\n   bead.\n\nEach of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The\ncurrent bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one\nattempt.\n\n## Recommendation to the orchestrator\n\nReopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into\nthe nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three\n(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated\ngoals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.\n\nThe reduced-scope language in the bead's own description (\"DDx-side\ntracker only\", \"blocks on a single upstream point release\") suggests\nthis bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as\nthe deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.\nresult_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nbase_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nretry_after=2026-04-29T04:30:22Z",

All six upstream routing-plan beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` are\n   `closed`:\n     agent-0dafc7f0, agent-191a74f9, agent-1f46cf22,\n     agent-53f38d95, agent-bab52778, agent-dfabb10b.\n\n2. Their closing commits are reachable from the tag DDx already pins:\n   `cli/go.mod` → `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.\n   `git merge-base --is-ancestor \u003cSHA\u003e v0.9.23` succeeds for the\n   closing commits of every routing-plan bead checked\n   (db26108, b645280, 0bc2bf4, b85e90e). So the upstream binary\n   surface AC #1 wants is already on the consumer's classpath; no\n   `go.mod` bump is even required.\n\n## Why this still cannot be a single-pass commit\n\nThe bead's AC enumerates 10 items spanning D1–D5 plus follow-ups:\n\n  - D1+D2: rip out DDx-side tier-ladder synthesis from the default\n    `ddx agent run` / `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-bead` paths so\n    each issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` call —\n    touches `workerDispatchAdapter`, `execute_bead_loop.go`,\n    `agent_cmd.go`, `profile_ladder.go`, `serviceconfig.go`.\n  - D3: insert a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before\n    `beadStore.Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~352`.\n  - D4: deliberate breaking config change — `default_harness` becomes\n    a hard error at load; `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides`\n    move to opt-in-only with one-time warnings, requires schema\n    deletion + migration test + CHANGELOG breaking-change note.\n  - D5: CLI override surface — flag plumbing + per-flag e2e tests\n    covering harness-only, model-only, harness+model conflict, and\n    profile+harness conflict.\n  - AC #6: Playwright drain-queue golden matching the user's real\n    endpoint config + a \"no gemini + non-gemini-model fallback\"\n    assertion.\n  - AC #7: search-and-delete pass for any DDx-side\n    allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring re-implementation, plus a\n    dead-code detector wired into CI to catch orphans of\n    `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef`.\n  - AC #8: file follow-up beads for D6 (route-status / providers UI)\n    and reaffirm `ddx-05b4cc9d` independence.\n  - AC #9: link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream docs and write\n    DDx CHANGELOG entry.\n  - AC #10: net-new regression integration test that reproduces the\n    19-burn drain-queue failure mode with the user's exact config —\n    requires fixture work (gemini binary presence, minimax override)\n    that is itself a sub-bead.\n\nConservatively: 1–3k LOC across ~10 Go files plus frontend Playwright\nfixtures plus CI wiring plus tracker mutations. Crucially D4 is a\ndeliberate breaking change to user-facing config schema; landing it\nwithout explicit user alignment risks breaking real `.ddx.yml` files\nin the wild. The bead description itself describes the work as \"one\nDDx PR\" but the AC list is the union of nine independent\ndeliverables.\n\nexecute-bead's contract is to satisfy AC deterministically, not to\nmake architecture or breaking-change decisions across nine\nsub-deliverables in one pass. A partial commit would fail most AC\nitems; a sprawling commit would couple breaking schema changes to\nmechanical refactors and frontend fixtures, which is exactly the\nshape of PR the bead's own sequencing tries to avoid.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt needs\n\nSplit this bead into the nine smaller beads listed in the prior\nattempt's rationale (`.ddx/executions/20260428T222708-3b6ff345/result.json`,\nsection \"What a follow-up attempt needs\"):\n\n  1. routing-bump (trivial; AC #1 — though arguably already\n     satisfied by v0.9.23)\n  2. routing-default-path (D1 + D2)\n  3. routing-preflight-gate (D3)\n  4. routing-config-deprecation (D4 — breaking, needs explicit\n     user sign-off in its own bead description)\n  5. routing-overrides-e2e (D5 + AC #5)\n  6. routing-drain-regression (AC #10)\n  7. routing-cleanup (AC #7 dead-code detector)\n  8. routing-frontend-goldens (AC #6)\n  9. routing-followups (AC #8)\n\nRecommendation: reopen this bead with helix-evolve to perform the\nsplit, keep ddx-fdd3ea36 as the epic tracker, and queue beads 2 and 3\nfirst — they unblock G1/G2/G3 with the smallest reasonable PR.\n\nThe reduced-scope language in the bead's own description (\"DDx-side\ntracker only\", \"blocks on a single upstream point release\") confirms\nthis was meant as an EPIC, not a deliverable. Two consecutive\nexecute-bead passes have now reached the same conclusion; further\nre-execution at higher model tiers will not change the outcome —\nthe blocker is bead shape, not capability.\nresult_rev=e4b048ebe768c8cf081ec745aebbddd23e73d786\nbase_rev=e4b048ebe768c8cf081ec745aebbddd23e73d786\nretry_after=2026-04-29T05:53:03Z",

.ddx/executions/20260429T003007-e136e75e/manifest.json:45:          "body": "no_changes\nrationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)\n\n## Decision\n\nStopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a\nmulti-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it\nin one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that\nfails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config\nschema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs\nwithout the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.\n\n## What is already done (verified in this worktree)\n\n1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under\n   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in\n   `closed` status:\n     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)\n     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)\n     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)\n     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)\n     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)\n     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)\n\n2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**\n   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.\n   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns\n   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)\n   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1\n   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.\n\n## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass\n\n### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative\n\nAC #1 asks for \"a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class\ndefault and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +\ngating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost\ndimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback.\"\n\nReading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:\n  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.\n  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.\n  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,\n    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent\n    \"routing point release\" narrative.\n\nThe closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as\nside-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the\ncombined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires\neither (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a \"v0.9.25 — routing\npoint release\" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead\ndescription must be relaxed to accept \"the code is in v0.9.23, even if\nno single CHANGELOG block summarizes it.\" Either resolution is a\ngovernance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.\n\n### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)\n\nThese criteria collectively require:\n  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one\n    `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` call with no Model / no Harness /\n    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;\n  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim\n    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);\n  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,\n    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,\n    with one-time process warnings;\n  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to\n    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;\n  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /\n    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with\n    dead-code detection in CI;\n  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn\n    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override\n    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness\n    conflict);\n  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's\n    real config;\n  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.\n\nThis is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:\n  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`\n  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`\n  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)\n  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)\n  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)\n  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)\n\nThat work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead\nexplicitly bundles into \"one DDx PR\" — but a PR of that size needs\narchitectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking\nconfig change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the\nfirst line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC\ndeterministically, not to make architecture decisions.\n\n### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads\n\nThe bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:\n  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,\n  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)\n    stays independent.\n\nFiling beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the\nproject's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is\nmechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.\n\n### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden\n\nA net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical\n19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test\n(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not\nhave gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt needs\n\nTo make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead\nmust be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:\n\n1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever\n   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add\n   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially\n   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.\n\n2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default\n   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:\n   \"default\")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.\n\n3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before\n   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded\n   incompatibility.\n\n4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on\n   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +\n   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.\n\n5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override\n   combination.\n\n6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test\n   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.\n\n7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of\n   compensating logic.\n\n8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.\n\n9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI\n   bead.\n\nEach of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The\ncurrent bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one\nattempt.\n\n## Recommendation to the orchestrator\n\nReopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into\nthe nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three\n(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated\ngoals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.\n\nThe reduced-scope language in the bead's own description (\"DDx-side\ntracker only\", \"blocks on a single upstream point release\") suggests\nthis bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as\nthe deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.\nresult_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nbase_rev=891112dee30444c8925bbc101abd3b1da50a3441\nretry_after=2026-04-29T04:30:22Z",

I re-verified the\nupstream / DDx state and concur with that prior analysis: the bead is a\nmulti-PR epic, not a single bounded execute-bead pass, and it should be\nsplit before another implementation attempt.\n\n## What is verified done\n\n1. All six upstream routing-plan beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` are\n   `closed`:\n     agent-0dafc7f0, agent-191a74f9, agent-1f46cf22,\n     agent-53f38d95, agent-bab52778, agent-dfabb10b.\n\n2. Their closing commits are reachable from the tag DDx already pins:\n   `cli/go.mod` → `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.\n   `git merge-base --is-ancestor \u003cSHA\u003e v0.9.23` succeeds for the\n   closing commits of every routing-plan bead checked\n   (db26108, b645280, 0bc2bf4, b85e90e). So the upstream binary\n   surface AC #1 wants is already on the consumer's classpath; no\n   `go.mod` bump is even required.\n\n## Why this still cannot be a single-pass commit\n\nThe bead's AC enumerates 10 items spanning D1–D5 plus follow-ups:\n\n  - D1+D2: rip out DDx-side tier-ladder synthesis from the default\n    `ddx agent run` / `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-bead` paths so\n    each issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: \"default\")` call —\n    touches `workerDispatchAdapter`, `execute_bead_loop.go`,\n    `agent_cmd.go`, `profile_ladder.go`, `serviceconfig.go`.\n  - D3: insert a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before\n    `beadStore.Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~352`.\n  - D4: deliberate breaking config change — `default_harness` becomes\n    a hard error at load; `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides`\n    move to opt-in-only with one-time warnings, requires schema\n    deletion + migration test + CHANGELOG breaking-change note.\n  - D5: CLI override surface — flag plumbing + per-flag e2e tests\n    covering harness-only, model-only, harness+model conflict, and\n    profile+harness conflict.\n  - AC #6: Playwright drain-queue golden matching the user's real\n    endpoint config + a \"no gemini + non-gemini-model fallback\"\n    assertion.\n  - AC #7: search-and-delete pass for any DDx-side\n    allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring re-implementation, plus a\n    dead-code detector wired into CI to catch orphans of\n    `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef`.\n  - AC #8: file follow-up beads for D6 (route-status / providers UI)\n    and reaffirm `ddx-05b4cc9d` independence.\n  - AC #9: link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream docs and write\n    DDx CHANGELOG entry.\n  - AC #10: net-new regression integration test that reproduces the\n    19-burn drain-queue failure mode with the user's exact config —\n    requires fixture work (gemini binary presence, minimax override)\n    that is itself a sub-bead.\n\nConservatively: 1–3k LOC across ~10 Go files plus frontend Playwright\nfixtures plus CI wiring plus tracker mutations. Crucially D4 is a\ndeliberate breaking change to user-facing config schema; landing it\nwithout explicit user alignment risks breaking real `.ddx.yml` files\nin the wild. The bead description itself describes the work as \"one\nDDx PR\" but the AC list is the union of nine independent\ndeliverables.\n\nexecute-bead's contract is to satisfy AC deterministically, not to\nmake architecture or breaking-change decisions across nine\nsub-deliverables in one pass. A partial commit would fail most AC\nitems; a sprawling commit would couple breaking schema changes to\nmechanical refactors and frontend fixtures, which is exactly the\nshape of PR the bead's own sequencing tries to avoid.\n\n## What a follow-up attempt needs\n\nSplit this bead into the nine smaller beads listed in the prior\nattempt's rationale (`.ddx/executions/20260428T222708-3b6ff345/result.json`,\nsection \"What a follow-up attempt needs\"):\n\n  1. routing-bump (trivial; AC #1 — though arguably already\n     satisfied by v0.9.23)\n  2. routing-default-path (D1 + D2)\n  3. routing-preflight-gate (D3)\n  4. routing-config-deprecation (D4 — breaking, needs explicit\n     user sign-off in its own bead description)\n  5. routing-overrides-e2e (D5 + AC #5)\n  6. routing-drain-regression (AC #10)\n  7. routing-cleanup (AC #7 dead-code detector)\n  8. routing-frontend-goldens (AC #6)\n  9. routing-followups (AC #8)\n\nRecommendation: reopen this bead with helix-evolve to perform the\nsplit, keep ddx-fdd3ea36 as the epic tracker, and queue beads 2 and 3\nfirst — they unblock G1/G2/G3 with the smallest reasonable PR.\n\nThe reduced-scope language in the bead's own description (\"DDx-side\ntracker only\", \"blocks on a single upstream point release\") confirms\nthis was meant as an EPIC, not a deliverable. Two consecutive\nexecute-bead passes have now reached the same conclusion; further\nre-execution at higher model tiers will not change the outcome —\nthe blocker is bead shape, not capability.\nresult_rev=e4b048ebe768c8cf081ec745aebbddd23e73d786\nbase_rev=e4b048ebe768c8cf081ec745aebbddd23e73d786\nretry_after=2026-04-29T05:53:03Z",

.ddx/executions/20260425T092410-e9991cad/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Upstream report (\"ddx execute-loop: post-merge reviewer silently fails on large prompts\", suggested fix #5) flags that even when the reviewer returns content, the markdown verdict extractor mis-parses it: when Claude responds with '### Verdict: APPROVE' the extractor pulls 'BLOCK' from the options header line in the prompt template that the model echoes back, logging '{\"verdict\":\"BLOCK\"}' for an actual APPROVE. This silently lets regressions land.\n\nFEAT-022 (parent ddx-0c35470e) is scoped to prompt assembly and size invariants; response parsing is out of scope there. File this as a sibling.\n\nSwitch the reviewer contract to a machine-readable JSON object emitted as the model's final response:\n\n  {\n    \"verdict\": \"APPROVE\" | \"REQUEST_CHANGES\" | \"BLOCK\",\n    \"findings\": [\n      { \"severity\": \"info\"|\"warn\"|\"block\", \"summary\": \"…\", \"location\": \"path:line?\" }\n    ],\n    \"summary\": \"≤300 char human-readable verdict justification\"\n  }\n\nReplace the existing markdown extractor with a strict JSON parser:\n1. Update the reviewer prompt template (post-collapse: agent.BuildReviewPrompt) to instruct the model to emit ONLY a JSON object matching the schema, fenced as ```json … ``` for robustness against models that won't emit raw JSON.\n2. Add internal/agent/review_verdict.go with a typed ReviewVerdict struct and a ParseReviewVerdict([]byte) (ReviewVerdict, error) that strips an optional ```json fence and decodes; reject on unknown verdict values.\n3. Replace the regex-based extractor call site in execute_bead_review.go with ParseReviewVerdict; on parse error, emit the existing 'unparseable' review-error class (introduced by ddx-70c1d2e2) — do NOT close the bead.\n4. Schema-version the contract: include schema_version: 1 in the prompt; the parser tolerates unknown fields but rejects unknown verdict values.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (prompt template, extractor call site)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go (new)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict_test.go (new)\n- prompt template asset under cli/internal/agent/assets/ if separated\n\nOut of scope:\n- Bounded retry / error-class taxonomy (ddx-70c1d2e2 owns this; this bead emits 'unparseable' into that taxonomy)\n- Prompt size invariants (FEAT-022)\n- Grading verdicts (separate bead if needed; this is review-only)\n\nCross-reference: This bead's `unparseable` emission feeds into ddx-70c1d2e2's bounded-retry logic. ddx-70c1d2e2 should be reviewed once both land to confirm the unparseable retry budget is sane (the upstream report observed real APPROVE verdicts being lost, so unparseable should retry, not terminal-escalate immediately).",
.ddx/executions/20260425T092410-e9991cad/manifest.json:35:    "worktree": "tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-021bd69b-20260425T092410-e9991cad"

.ddx/executions/20260425T092410-e9991cad/prompt.md:5:Upstream report ("ddx execute-loop: post-merge reviewer silently fails on large prompts", suggested fix #5) flags that even when the reviewer returns content, the markdown verdict extractor mis-parses it: when Claude responds with '### Verdict: APPROVE' the extractor pulls 'BLOCK' from the options header line in the prompt template that the model echoes back, logging '{"verdict":"BLOCK"}' for an actual APPROVE. This silently lets regressions land.

FEAT-022 (parent ddx-0c35470e) is scoped to prompt assembly and size invariants; response parsing is out of scope there. File this as a sibling.

Switch the reviewer contract to a machine-readable JSON object emitted as the model's final response:

  {
    "verdict": "APPROVE" | "REQUEST_CHANGES" | "BLOCK",
    "findings": [
      { "severity": "info"|"warn"|"block", "summary": "…", "location": "path:line?" }
    ],
    "summary": "≤300 char human-readable verdict justification"
  }

Replace the existing markdown extractor with a strict JSON parser:
1. Update the reviewer prompt template (post-collapse: agent.BuildReviewPrompt) to instruct the model to emit ONLY a JSON object matching the schema, fenced as ```json … ``` for robustness against models that won't emit raw JSON.
2. Add internal/agent/review_verdict.go with a typed ReviewVerdict struct and a ParseReviewVerdict([]byte) (ReviewVerdict, error) that strips an optional ```json fence and decodes; reject on unknown verdict values.
3. Replace the regex-based extractor call site in execute_bead_review.go with ParseReviewVerdict; on parse error, emit the existing 'unparseable' review-error class (introduced by ddx-70c1d2e2) — do NOT close the bead.
4. Schema-version the contract: include schema_version: 1 in the prompt; the parser tolerates unknown fields but rejects unknown verdict values.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (prompt template, extractor call site)
- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go (new)
- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict_test.go (new)
- prompt template asset under cli/internal/agent/assets/ if separated

Out of scope:
- Bounded retry / error-class taxonomy (ddx-70c1d2e2 owns this; this bead emits 'unparseable' into that taxonomy)
- Prompt size invariants (FEAT-022)
- Grading verdicts (separate bead if needed; this is review-only)

Cross-reference: This bead's `unparseable` emission feeds into ddx-70c1d2e2's bounded-retry logic. ddx-70c1d2e2 should be reviewed once both land to confirm the unparseable retry budget is sane (the upstream report observed real APPROVE verdicts being lost, so unparseable should retry, not terminal-escalate immediately).

.ddx/executions/20260425T084842-dd955bd9/prompt.md:5:Stage G of FEAT-022. Split review-error into four explicit outcome
classes. Add a bounded retry policy per result_rev with terminal
manual-escalation event. Preserve the invariant that reviewer failure
does NOT close the bead.

Implements FEAT-022 requirements §12, §13, §14. Satisfies US-222.

Depends on Stage C1 (ddx-12934d66) — the context_overflow class is
emitted by Stage C1's pre-dispatch short-circuit; this bead formalizes
the full taxonomy and the retry counter logic.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (classify errors returned
  from the review dispatch into the four taxonomy classes per §12:
  context_overflow, provider_empty, unparseable, transport)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (count review attempts per
  result_rev via existing bead events; on Nth failure emit terminal
  review-manual-required event and do NOT re-execute primary; a new
  result_rev resets the counter; iterations without a result_rev
  (--no-merge, execution-failed) do NOT consume the budget per FEAT-022
  §14)
- cli/internal/config or per-project config (add a review_max_retries
  field — default 3)
- Tests: each of the four error classes triggers from its distinct
  root cause (empty output, unparseable output, context overflow from
  Stage C1, transport failure); retry counter scoping (result_rev
  change resets, --no-merge does not consume budget); terminal
  review-manual-required event after N failures; bead is NOT closed by
  the reviewer failure path (invariant from
  execute_bead_review_failure_modes_test.go:13 stays green).

Out-of-scope:
- Any change that could auto-close a bead on reviewer failure (§13
  invariant is non-negotiable)
- UI for surfacing review-manual-required beads (separate work)
- Context-overflow escalation to a smaller reviewer model or tighter
  caps on retry (noted as follow-up in FEAT-022, not in scope here)

The terminal 'review-manual-required' event body should include the
failure class, attempt count, and the result_rev being blocked, so
operators can triage without opening multiple bundles.

.ddx/executions/20260425T084842-dd955bd9/prompt.md:17:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. ## How to work

.ddx/executions/20260425T073541-4f46941d/prompt.md:11:## 2026-04-22 — User directives on fixture size and storage strategy

Two standing instructions from the user that apply to this bead and to all other perf beads (`ddx-ad0db8fd`, `ddx-2ceb02fa`, and any spawned from efficacy/providers work):

1. **Minimum fixture size: 1,000s of records.** The original acceptance criterion (10 projects × 500 beads = 5,000) already meets this for beads, but restate the floor explicitly: **no perf work lands against a fixture smaller than 1,000 records per query shape**. Sessions fixtures, efficacy fixtures, provider/quota history fixtures — all ≥1k rows. Beads fixture stays at 5k; sessions fixture for `ddx-2ceb02fa` should be ≥10k index entries (already specified); efficacy and any new surfaces ≥1k attempts/rows each.

2. **DB backend is on the table.** If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, lightweight index) cannot hit the target latencies on the fixture sizes above, the user is open to introducing a real data store — candidates named: **axon**, **sqlite**, **postgres**. Do not silently pick one: **raise the decision with the user before choosing a path.** Capture the baseline, capture what in-place optimization achieved, then bring the choice (and the rationale) to the user.

These directives supersede any acceptance criterion that implies "optimize in place no matter what." If the numbers say storage needs to change, that's the outcome — but the user decides the substrate.

**AC §8 — Playwright cross-project test routes wrong path**: `beads-smoke.spec.ts:107` navigates to `/nodes/${NODE_INFO.id}/projects/${PROJECT_ID}/beads` for the "cross-project" test, which is the same per-project URL as the 1s test. A cross-project smoke test should hit the all-beads route (e.g., `/nodes/${NODE_INFO.id}/beads` or whatever the actual cross-project URL is) and mock the `beads` query, not `beadsByProject`. The mock for `beads` exists (lines 76-89) but is never exercised because both tests navigate to the project-scoped path.
**AC §9 — Bead notes cross-reference**: The acceptance criteria require this bead's notes to list `ddx-ad0db8fd` and `ddx-2ceb02fa`. The baseline `.md` and code comments mention them, but no `ddx bead update` call or notes update appears in the diff. If the notes were updated via a separate mechanism not captured in this branch, this finding can be dismissed.

## 2026-04-23 — Baseline and perf-thread cross-reference

Baseline recorded in `docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/2026-04-22-baseline.md` and JSON sibling from the 10 projects x 500 beads fixture (5,000 beads total, 1,000 session rows). Environment: `go=go1.26.2 goos=linux goarch=arm64 cpu-cores=12`.

Key p95 results: `bead` 6.72ms in-process / 6.80ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 8.84ms in-process / 9.29ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 62.16ms in-process / 75.80ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.45ms in-process / 6.87ms HTTP.

Cross-reference for inherited harness work: `ddx-ad0db8fd` covers single-bead detail lookup; `ddx-2ceb02fa` covers sessions shard/index work. Both should use/extend this GraphQL perf harness.

Review follow-up: AC §8 cross-project Playwright smoke now navigates to `/nodes/{nodeId}/beads` and exercises the `beads` GraphQL query instead of the project-scoped `beadsByProject` path. Proposed CI posture remains a follow-up +25% p95 regression gate, not implemented in this bead.

## 2026-04-23 — Baseline refresh after session-index fixture alignment

Re-ran `BENCH_REPORT_DATE=2026-04-22 make bench-graphql` after updating the perf fixture to seed current pointer-only session-index shards instead of legacy `sessions.jsonl`. Refreshed artifacts: `docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/2026-04-22-baseline.md` and `.json`.

Refreshed p95 results: `bead` 7.23ms in-process / 7.29ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 7.18ms in-process / 7.83ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 62.49ms in-process / 67.29ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.74ms in-process / 7.73ms HTTP; `agentSessions` 13.45ms in-process / 14.19ms HTTP.

## 2026-04-23 — Final `make bench-graphql` verification

After fixing `cli/Makefile` to pass an absolute `DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR`, `BENCH_REPORT_DATE=2026-04-22 make bench-graphql` writes to the intended repo docs path. Final p95 results from that run: `bead` 6.76ms in-process / 7.20ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 6.88ms in-process / 7.85ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 63.22ms in-process / 71.82ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.75ms in-process / 7.56ms HTTP; `agentSessions` 13.24ms in-process / 13.64ms HTTP.

.ddx/executions/20260425T111752-c108bda9/prompt.md:17:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260425T111752-c108bda9/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.

.ddx/executions/20260424T052224-c4bd8312/prompt.md:17:You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260424T052224-c4bd8312/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.

Implements FEAT-022 requirements §12, §13, §14. Satisfies US-222.

Depends on Stage C1 (ddx-12934d66) — the context_overflow class is
emitted by Stage C1's pre-dispatch short-circuit; this bead formalizes
the full taxonomy and the retry counter logic.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (classify errors returned
  from the review dispatch into the four taxonomy classes per §12:
  context_overflow, provider_empty, unparseable, transport)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (count review attempts per
  result_rev via existing bead events; on Nth failure emit terminal
  review-manual-required event and do NOT re-execute primary; a new
  result_rev resets the counter; iterations without a result_rev
  (--no-merge, execution-failed) do NOT consume the budget per FEAT-022
  §14)
- cli/internal/config or per-project config (add a review_max_retries
  field — default 3)
- Tests: each of the four error classes triggers from its distinct
  root cause (empty output, unparseable output, context overflow from
  Stage C1, transport failure); retry counter scoping (result_rev
  change resets, --no-merge does not consume budget); terminal
  review-manual-required event after N failures; bead is NOT closed by
  the reviewer failure path (invariant from
  execute_bead_review_failure_modes_test.go:13 stays green).

Out-of-scope:
- Any change that could auto-close a bead on reviewer failure (§13
  invariant is non-negotiable)
- UI for surfacing review-manual-required beads (separate work)
- Context-overflow escalation to a smaller reviewer model or tighter
  caps on retry (noted as follow-up in FEAT-022, not in scope here)

The terminal 'review-manual-required' event body should include the
failure class, attempt count, and the result_rev being blocked, so
operators can triage without opening multiple bundles.

Each subtest asserts the emitted review-error event body contains the literal class identifier. cd cli && go test -run TestBoundedReviewRetry ./internal/agent/... passes with three cases: (a) after N (default 3) failures on the same result_rev, the bead emits a review-manual-required event and a subsequent execute-loop iteration does NOT re-execute primary; (b) a fresh result_rev resets the counter; (c) iterations that do NOT produce a committed result_rev do NOT increment the counter, verified explicitly for both --no-merge iterations AND execution-failed iterations. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... includes execute_bead_review_failure_modes_test.go and passes unchanged (§13 invariant — reviewer failure does NOT close the bead). Default review_max_retries = 3; cd cli && go test -run TestReviewMaxRetriesConfig ./internal/config/... passes with a test that overrides the value in .ddx/config.yaml and verifies the loop respects the override. .ddx/executions/20260418T060325-e4e5ad24/prompt.md:5:The embedded ddx-agent harness honors the per-run SessionLogDir override (agent_runner.go:136-138: `logDir := opts.SessionLogDir; if empty fall back to r.Config.SessionLogDir`) so that execute-bead runs drop their JSONL trace inside the bundle at .ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl.

The claude harness does NOT honor that override. claude_stream.go:327 reads only r.Config.SessionLogDir, so every claude execute-bead run writes its trace into the runner-wide default (`.ddx/agent-logs/`) and the bundle's embedded/ dir stays empty. execute_bead.go:446 already passes SessionLogDir: embeddedStateDir through RunOptions; it just isn't used by the claude path.

Empirical evidence from axon's 2026-04-15 ADR-018 session: 2 runs with `harness: agent` produced 34-51 MB traces in embedded/; 23 runs with `harness: claude` left embedded/ empty while `.ddx/agent-logs/` grew to 162 MB.

Fix: plumb opts.SessionLogDir into runClaudeStreaming (either accept it as a parameter or read it off resolvedOpts) and prefer it over r.Config.SessionLogDir when non-empty. Matches the embedded harness behavior exactly.

Parity matters: users want per-run forensic traces attached to the bundle regardless of which harness ran the bead.
